zh-tw.title: 冷笑話大全
zh-tw.description: 精選各種讓人會心一笑的冷笑話，適合各種場合破冰使用

en.title: Cold Jokes Collection
en.description: A curated collection of groan-worthy cold jokes perfect for breaking the ice

zh-tw.content:
小明問媽媽：「為什麼豆腐不會迷路？」
媽媽說不知道。
小明：「因為它是『嫩』豆腐啊，走哪都有人扶！」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在家庭聚餐時講
variations: 也可以改成問老師版本

en.content:
Tom asked his mom: "Why does tofu never get lost?"
Mom: "I don't know."
Tom: "Because it's so soft that everyone holds it up!"
en.editorNote: Good for family dinners
variations: Try the teacher version: same question, same punchline — just have Tom ask his science teacher instead of his mom.

zh-tw.content:
你知道企鵝為什麼不怕被北極熊吃掉嗎？

因為企鵝住在南極，北極熊住在北極，他們根本遇不到！
zh-tw.editorNote: 也是一個冷知識
variations: 可以接著問：那為什麼北極熊不搬去南極？因為簽證辦不下來

en.content:
Do you know why penguins aren't afraid of polar bears?

Because penguins live in the South Pole and polar bears live in the North Pole. They never meet!
en.editorNote: Also a fun fact
variations: Follow up: Why don't polar bears move south? Visa issues.

zh-tw.content:
阿華去看醫生，醫生說：「你的肝不好。」
阿華：「那我可以換一個好一點的嗎？」
醫生：「...你以為這是手機換殼嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 看醫生系列冷笑話
variations: 也可以換成腎臟版：「你的腎功能不太好。」「那我可以訂購一個新的嗎？」「……你以為這是電商網站？」

en.content:
Jake went to see a doctor. The doctor said: "Your liver isn't doing well."
Jake: "Can I get a better one?"
Doctor: "...This isn't a phone case swap."
en.editorNote: Doctor visit humor
variations: Try the kidney version: 'Your kidney function is low.' 'Can I just order a replacement?' '...This isn't Amazon.'

zh-tw.content:
老師問小美：「一加一等於多少？」
小美：「不知道。」
老師：「回家問爸爸媽媽。」
小美回家問媽媽，媽媽正在煮菜：「別煩我！」
問爸爸，爸爸正在看球賽：「好球！」
隔天老師問小美，小美說：「別煩我，好球！」
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典小學生系列
variations: 換成阿嬤版：阿嬤在追劇一直說「慘了！」，隔天小美告訴老師：「一加一等於慘了！」

en.content:
Teacher asks Lily: "What's one plus one?"
Lily: "I don't know."
Teacher: "Go ask your parents."
Lily asks mom, who's cooking: "Don't bother me!"
Asks dad, who's watching sports: "Goal!"
Next day, teacher asks again. Lily: "Don't bother me, goal!"
en.editorNote: Classic schoolkid joke
variations: Grandma version: grandma watching her drama keeps gasping 'Oh no!' all evening. Next morning, Lily answers: 'One plus one is Oh no!'

zh-tw.content:
為什麼螃蟹都不分享東西？

因為牠們都很「自蟹」（自私）！
zh-tw.editorNote: 諧音梗愛好者必備
variations: 為什麼蝦子很大方？因為牠們都很「蝦」趴（瞎趴）

en.content:
Why don't crabs ever share?

Because they're shellfish (selfish)!
en.editorNote: Classic English pun
variations: Why are shrimp so generous? They always come out of their shell.

zh-tw.content:
你知道什麼魚最白費力氣嗎？

白帶魚。

因為牠游了一輩子，走到最後還是「白帶」著走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 諧音梗加冷知識，海鮮愛好者一定笑得出來
variations: 什麼魚最省力？懶魚——因為牠什麼都懶得做，還不是活得好好的

en.content:
Which fish works hardest for nothing?

A conveyor belt fish — it keeps moving but never gets anywhere.
en.editorNote: Deadpan delivery works best for this one
variations: What fish never finishes anything? A procrastinating salmon — always upstream but never arriving.

zh-tw.content:
醫生說：「每天吃魚對腦子很好。」
小美認真執行，一個月後回診。
醫生問：「有沒有感覺比較聰明了？」
小美說：「有，我終於想清楚一件事。」
醫生：「什麼事？」
小美：「下次不要再來看你了，太貴了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 診所候診時講給旁邊的人聽，效果一流
variations: 也可以換成：「我終於想清楚了，以後自己上網查就好」

en.content:
Doctor: "Eating fish every day improves your brain."
Meg followed the advice for a month and came back for a checkup.
Doctor: "Feeling smarter?"
Meg: "Actually, yes. I figured something out."
Doctor: "Oh? What's that?"
Meg: "That I should stop coming here. Too expensive."
en.editorNote: Great for waiting rooms — tell the person next to you
variations: Alternate punchline: 'That I can just Google my symptoms for free.'

zh-tw.content:
你知道為什麼公車永遠都比預定時間晚到嗎？

因為它是「公」車，講求公平原則——讓每個人都等一樣久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 等公車的時候講給旁邊的人聽，一起等比較不無聊
variations: 捷運為什麼比較準時？因為它是私人地下空間，不用對大眾負責

en.content:
Why is the bus always late?

Because it's a public service — it believes in equal treatment. Everyone waits the same amount of time.
en.editorNote: Say this out loud while waiting at the bus stop. Instant bus stop friends.
variations: Why is the subway more punctual? Because it runs underground — out of sight, out of schedule.

zh-tw.content:
「貓咪下雨天最喜歡做什麼？」
「什麼？」
「等雨停。」
「……就這樣？」
「對，因為牠不想變成落湯貓。牠很清楚自己的底線。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 配上一臉認真的表情講，效果更好
variations: 狗狗下雨天喜歡做什麼？衝出去淋雨——牠完全沒有底線

en.content:
"What does a cat love to do on a rainy day?"
"What?"
"Wait for it to stop."
"...That's it?"
"Yes. Because it knows its limits. A wet cat is an undignified cat."
en.editorNote: Deliver this completely seriously for maximum effect
variations: What does a dog love to do on a rainy day? Run straight into it. No limits whatsoever.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道冰淇淋為什麼從來不吵架嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為它一開口就化掉了，話都說不完。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 夏天吃冰的時候講，對方如果笑了就是懂幽默的人
variations: 冰淇淋為什麼不生氣？因為它心態一直是甜的，來不及變酸

en.content:
"Do you know why ice cream never argues?"
"Why?"
"Because the moment it opens up, it melts before finishing the sentence."
en.editorNote: Say this while eating ice cream in summer for full effect
variations: Why is ice cream never bitter? Because it doesn't have time to turn sour — it's already gone.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道手機充電很慢的時候最像哪種人嗎？」
「像誰？」
「像那種說『好了好了，快了快了』，但五分鐘過去只多了2%的人。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 等充電的時候說給旁邊的人聽，引發深刻共鳴
variations: 有時候手機充電線比手機本人還難搞：插了半天沒反應，拔掉重插才有效

en.content:
"You know what a slow-charging phone reminds me of?"
"What?"
"The person who keeps saying 'almost ready!' but five minutes later they've only moved 2%."
en.editorNote: Say this while waiting for your phone to charge. Instant commiseration.
variations: Sometimes the cable is more difficult than the phone — wiggle it for five minutes and it still pretends not to notice you.

zh-tw.content:
「為什麼蝸牛爬得那麼慢，還是有很多粉絲？」
「因為牠慢慢爬，大家都以為牠在思考什麼深刻的事。」
「那牠在想什麼？」
「牠在想：為什麼我背著這麼重的殼還要繼續爬。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 人生哲理和冷笑話的完美結合，適合週一早上分享
variations: 蝸牛說：我不是慢，我是在享受過程。其他動物：你連終點在哪都不知道。

en.content:
"Why does a snail move so slowly but still have so many admirers?"
"Because people assume it's deep in thought."
"What is it actually thinking?"
"Why am I still carrying this shell up this hill."
en.editorNote: Philosophy and humor in one — perfect for a Monday morning
variations: Snail's life motto: 'I'm not slow. I'm savoring the journey.' Other animals: 'You don't even know where you're going.'

zh-tw.content:
小明去菜市場買菜，看到招牌寫著：「今日特價：買一送一」。
小明開心地買了一把蔥。
回到家，媽媽問：「你怎麼只買一把？不是買一送一嗎？」
小明：「對啊，我買一把，老闆送我一把，所以我把那把還給他了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在家人聚餐的場合講，長輩通常會先沉默兩秒再笑
variations: 延伸版：小明買一送一的衛生紙，把送的那包退給老闆說：「我家只需要一包。」

en.content:
Alex went to the grocery store and saw a sign: "Today's special: buy one, get one free."
He happily bought one bag of chips.
At home, his mom asked: "Why only one bag? Wasn't it buy one get one?"
Alex: "Right — I bought one, they gave me one free, so I gave that one back. I only needed one."
en.editorNote: Tell this to older relatives at dinner. Expect two seconds of silence before the laugh.
variations: Extended: Alex returned the free roll of paper towels saying 'We only need one roll at home, keep the extra.'

zh-tw.content:
你知道剪刀走路為什麼特別有自信嗎？

因為不管去哪裡，它都知道自己能「剪」出一條路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 諧音加上正能量包裝，適合在需要打氣的場合說完後補一句「你也一樣」
variations: 剪刀從來不怕迷路，因為走到哪都能開路

en.content:
Why does a pair of scissors always walk with such confidence?

Because no matter where it goes, it knows it can cut through.
en.editorNote: Deliver it straight, then optionally add: 'You're the same way.'
variations: Scissors never worry about dead ends — they just make a new path.

zh-tw.content:
小傑說：「我設了五個鬧鐘，保證早起。」
朋友問：「那你幾點起來的？」
小傑：「第六個響的時候。」
朋友：「你不是說只有五個？」
小傑：「第六個是我媽打來的電話。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 睡眠障礙族的自我介紹，傳給那個每天說明天一定要早起的朋友
variations: 七個鬧鐘都沒用，有時候唯一有效的還是媽媽的奪命連環call

en.content:
Jamie said: "I set five alarms to make sure I wake up early."
Friend: "So what time did you get up?"
Jamie: "When the sixth one went off."
Friend: "You said you only set five."
Jamie: "The sixth one was my mom calling."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who says they'll sleep early 'starting tomorrow'
variations: Five alarms, a sunrise playlist, and a motivational poster — no match for a mom's missed call notification.

zh-tw.content:
為什麼章魚是最理想的好朋友？

因為你不管拜託它幾件事，它永遠有足夠的手。
zh-tw.editorNote: 朋友需要幫忙的時候說，笑完了順便讓對方感受到你很願意伸出手
variations: 如果人類有八隻手，多工處理可能還是不夠——因為任務清單也會變八倍長

en.content:
Why is an octopus the ideal best friend?

Because no matter how many things you ask for help with, it always has enough hands.
en.editorNote: Say this when a friend asks for a favor — a funny way to say you're there for them
variations: If humans had eight arms, the to-do list would just grow eight times longer.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道吐司麵包為什麼每天都很開心嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為不管今天過得怎樣，它都被『烤』得很好。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 早餐的時候說，旁邊的人如果正好在吃吐司，效果加倍
variations: 吐司人生哲學：被高溫考驗之後，才能變得酥脆有層次

en.content:
"Do you know why toast is always in a good mood?"
"Why?"
"Because no matter what kind of day it's having, it always comes out well-toasted."
en.editorNote: Best delivered at breakfast, especially if someone is literally eating toast
variations: Toast life philosophy: only after enduring the heat do you become something worth spreading on.

zh-tw.content:
你知道熊貓為什麼特別省錢嗎？

因為牠的衣服從出生就不用換了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 動物園或看熊貓影片時說，大人小孩都能笑到
variations: 斑馬也一樣省事：一套西裝穿到底，還是條紋款，永遠不退流行

en.content:
Do you know why pandas are so thrifty?

Because they've been wearing the same outfit since birth.
en.editorNote: Say this at the zoo or while watching a panda video — works on all ages
variations: Zebras are the same: one striped suit, forever on trend, zero wardrobe budget.

zh-tw.content:
醫生跟病人說：「你要多喝水。」
病人：「我每天都有喝啊。」
醫生：「你一天喝幾杯？」
病人：「珍珠奶茶兩杯，美式一杯。」
醫生：「……我指的是開水。」
病人：「那個不就是水嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給每天用飲料代替水的朋友，他會笑完後默默去倒一杯水
variations: 升級版：「那我以後買無糖奶茶，算不算健康水？」醫生：「……下一位。」

en.content:
Doctor: "You need to drink more water."
Patient: "I drink plenty every day."
Doctor: "How many cups?"
Patient: "Two bubble teas and one iced Americano."
Doctor: "I meant plain water."
Patient: "Isn't that also water?"
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who treats boba as hydration — they'll laugh, then feel seen
variations: Upgrade: 'What if I get unsweetened tea — does that count?' Doctor: '...Next patient.'

zh-tw.content:
你知道魚為什麼從來不抱怨天氣嗎？

因為牠住在水裡，不管外面晴天還是下雨，對牠來說感覺都差不多。

這就叫做：活在自己的世界裡，心情特別穩定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 天氣不好有人一直抱怨的時候講這個，對方不是笑就是白眼
variations: 烏龜也一樣——背著殼走到哪裡都是家，哪裡有什麼好抱怨的

en.content:
Do you know why fish never complain about the weather?

Because they live underwater — whether it's sunny or pouring outside, it all feels about the same to them.

That's what you call: building your own world and staying calm.
en.editorNote: Say this when someone won't stop complaining about the weather — works either way
variations: Tortoises have the same energy — home is wherever they go, so what's there to complain about?

zh-tw.content:
你知道泡麵最厲害的地方是什麼嗎？

不管你今天過得多糟，三分鐘後它永遠準時出現在你面前。

比任何朋友都守時，比任何外送都快。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜加班、考試前或失戀時說，絕對引發深刻共鳴
variations: 泡麵從不遲到，從不說『好了好了，快了』，永遠是三分鐘——或是你等不及的兩分半鐘

en.content:
Do you know the most impressive thing about instant noodles?

No matter how bad your day was, they show up — ready — exactly three minutes later.

More punctual than any friend. Faster than any delivery.
en.editorNote: Say this during a late work night, exam week, or heartbreak — instant relatability
variations: Instant noodles never say 'almost ready.' Three minutes. Every time. More reliable than most people.

zh-tw.content:
你知道竹子為什麼特別有氣質嗎？

因為它做什麼都是「有節」的。

有節操、有節氣、有節制——一根竹子，活出了很多人一輩子都學不會的三件事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在文藝氣息濃厚的場合說，或是當成給自己的提醒
variations: 蓮花也很厲害：出淤泥而不染，連住的地方都選那麼有哲學

en.content:
Why does bamboo always seem so dignified?

Because everything it does comes from the joints — it has structure, integrity, and restraint built right in.

One plant, embodying three things most people spend a lifetime trying to figure out.
en.editorNote: Works as a reflective observation or a subtle self-reminder — say it slowly and meaningfully
variations: The lotus has it figured out too: growing in mud, but refusing to be anything like it.

zh-tw.content:
為什麼鴕鳥遇到危險的時候把頭埋進沙子裡？

因為牠認為：「我看不到危險，代表危險也看不到我。」

這個邏輯在鴕鳥界極具說服力，且被廣泛應用。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個遇到問題習慣當作沒看到的朋友，牠懂的
variations: 人類版：把未讀通知關掉——如果我不知道，問題就不存在。這個邏輯與鴕鳥如出一轍。

en.content:
Why does an ostrich bury its head in the sand when danger approaches?

Because it firmly believes: "If I can't see the threat, the threat can't see me."

This logic is deeply compelling in ostrich circles and widely practiced.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who ignores problems hoping they'll disappear — they'll recognize themselves
variations: Human version: turning off notification badges. If I don't see it, it technically doesn't exist yet. Same logic, different species.

zh-tw.content:
小傑在超市收銀台前猶豫很久。
收銀員：「先生，你要排隊。」
小傑：「我在等看看有沒有比較短的隊。」
收銀員：「那你去別隊就好了。」
小傑：「但如果我去別隊，這隊有可能就變最短了。」
收銀員：「……你願意等多久？」
小傑：「視情況而定。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在超市排隊的時候說給旁邊的人聽，引發集體共鳴
variations: 台灣特定版本：捷運閘門前換了三次閘口，最後還是選錯了那條

en.content:
Sam hesitated for a long time at the supermarket checkout.
Cashier: "Sir, you need to get in line."
Sam: "I'm waiting to see if a shorter line appears."
Cashier: "Then just go to a different line."
Sam: "But if I leave this one, it might become the shortest."
Cashier: "...How long are you willing to wait?"
Sam: "Depends on the data."
en.editorNote: Tell this at a checkout line — the person next to you will relate immediately
variations: Airport security version: switched lanes three times and ended up in the slowest one of all.

zh-tw.content:
你知道麵條為什麼從來不難過嗎？

因為不管發生什麼事，牠都是「樂在其『麵』」。

而且就算被燙到、被切斷，最後還是要被人好好對待——因為大家都要吃它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 吃麵的時候說，對方如果是愛吃麵的人，會笑得特別開心
variations: 義大利麵版：漂洋過海、被沸水煮、被叉子捲——但人生目標始終明確：被好好吃掉

en.content:
Why are noodles never sad?

Because no matter what happens to them — boiled, tangled, or cut in half — they always end up being treated well by someone who really wanted them.

Life goals, honestly.
en.editorNote: Say this while eating noodles with someone who loves them — works especially well mid-bite
variations: Pasta variation: crossed an ocean, got boiled alive, tossed around in a pan — still ended up on someone's favorite-food list.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道仙人掌為什麼這麼受歡迎嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為它不需要你的愛，不需要你記得澆水，也不需要你特別關注——但它就是活得好好的。」
「……這是在說仙人掌嗎？」
「是，也可以不是。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個號稱「超好養」的朋友，或是在辦公室角落盆栽旁邊說，效果極佳
variations: 仙人掌的人生哲學：存在感不高，但扎人的時候讓人印象深刻。

en.content:
"Do you know why cacti are so popular?"
"Why?"
"Because they don't need your love, don't need you to remember to water them, and don't need any special attention — and they thrive anyway."
"...Are we still talking about cacti?"
"Yes. Could also be someone you know."
en.editorNote: Share next to the office plant, or send to that friend who claims they're 'very low-maintenance'
variations: Cactus life philosophy: low presence, but when they do make an impression, it sticks.

zh-tw.content:
你知道雨傘最可憐的地方是什麼嗎？

它最被需要的那一天，通常是你忘在家裡的那天。

雨傘的一生，就是在家等你，然後在你出門之後，發現自己不在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下雨天說，或是傳給忘記帶傘被淋濕的朋友，他會笑完繼續淋
variations: 雨傘的最高境界：被帶出去但沒下雨，然後被遺留在咖啡廳——這叫做犧牲自我，成就別人的空手而歸。

en.content:
Do you know the saddest thing about umbrellas?

The day they're needed most is usually the day you left them at home.

An umbrella's whole life: waiting at home, then realizing it's not where it needs to be — after you've already left.
en.editorNote: Say this on a rainy day, or send to the friend who just got soaked — they'll laugh and keep walking
variations: Peak umbrella tragedy: brought out on a sunny day, then forgotten at a coffee shop. A sacrifice for nothing.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道鬧鐘最大的謊言是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「上面的數字。它說07:00，但人類聽到的是：再五分鐘。
然後是另一個再五分鐘。
七點二十分，鬧鐘已經說了四次真話，但沒有一次被認真對待。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個永遠說明天一定要早起的朋友，他看完會在07:26分傳你一個哭笑表情
variations: 鬧鐘的悲哀：它說的永遠是事實，但只有人類決定事實算不算數。

en.content:
"Do you know the biggest lie an alarm clock tells?"
"What?"
"The numbers. It says 07:00, but what humans hear is: five more minutes.
Then another five.
By 7:20, the alarm has told the truth four times — and been ignored every single one."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always says they'll sleep early 'starting tomorrow' — they'll respond at 7:26am with a crying emoji
variations: The alarm's tragedy: it always tells the truth. Humans just decide whether the truth counts.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道豆干為什麼特別有韌性嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為不管被切、被燙、被醃、被炸，牠最後都能好好出現在你面前。
這就是豆干的精神——被處理了很多次，還是自己。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 吃鹹水雞或滷味的時候說，對方通常會先笑再繼續夾
variations: 豆干語錄：經歷越多，越入味。這可以是食物的道理，也可以是人的道理。

en.content:
"Do you know why firm tofu has such resilience?"
"Why?"
"Because no matter how many times it's sliced, boiled, marinated, or fried, it still shows up at the table as itself.
That's the spirit of firm tofu — processed many times, still recognizably itself."
en.editorNote: Say this while eating braised snacks or hot pot — they'll laugh, then keep picking up pieces
variations: Firm tofu wisdom: the more it goes through, the more flavor it absorbs. That applies to food. And to people.

items.sourceNote: I人/E人梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
I人的浪漫是這樣的：

明明很想傳訊息給你，
打了三遍，刪了三遍，
最後傳了一個句號。

你回：「你還好嗎？」

I人心裡：好，只是很難主動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個I人朋友，他看了會說「這說的不就是我」，也適合E人看懂身邊I人的表達方式
variations: I人的告白：對話框開了又關，關了又開，最後傳了一個：「今天天氣不錯」——其實已經鼓起了很大勇氣。
I人的告白：打了五百字，傳出去的只有「嗯」。

en.content:
How an introvert shows they care:

They really want to text you.
They type it out, delete it. Three times.
Finally they send a single period.

You reply: "Are you okay?"

Introvert internally: Yes. That period was my bravest act today.
en.editorNote: Send to your introvert friend — they'll say 'this is literally me.' Also useful for extroverts trying to understand the introverts in their life.
variations: Introvert's confession: they opened the chat, closed it, opened it again, then sent: 'Nice weather today.' That was a love letter.
Introvert love confession: typed 500 words, sent 'ok'.

items.sourceNote: I人/E人梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
I人最崩潰的一句話：
「剛好你也在！我順便把大家介紹給你認識吧。」

聽到這句話的I人：
臉上：微笑。
心裡：已經在評估逃生路線了。

這叫做社交體力的緊急透支。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給E人朋友，讓他理解為什麼I人聚會後需要一個人待著充電
variations: E人進階版：「我叫大家都來，到時候你不用擔心沒話說，我幫你接！」——I人心裡：……謝謝，但我想回家了。

en.content:
The phrase that sends every introvert into quiet panic:
"Oh, you're here too! Let me introduce you to everyone."

Introvert's face: smiling.
Introvert's mind: silently calculating all possible exit routes.

This is called a social energy emergency overdraft.
en.editorNote: Share with your extrovert friend who still doesn't understand why introverts need 'recharge time' after gatherings
variations: Advanced extrovert move: 'I invited everyone — don't worry, I'll carry the conversation for you!' Introvert: ...I need to go home now.

items.sourceNote: I人電梯困境，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
I人的日常高難度情境：
電梯門打開，裡面站著一個認識的人。

兩個人都立刻看手機。
兩個人都知道對方看到自己了。
都在等對方先開口。

電梯抵達，齊聲說：「你先走，你先走。」

這次互動讓雙方消耗了比爬十層樓更多的精力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給任何I人，他會轉傳給所有認識的I人，然後大家沉默地點頭
variations: I人的終極噩夢：搭電梯遇到認識的人，而且電梯很慢，還多停了兩層——社交體力當場歸零。

en.content:
High-difficulty daily situation for introverts:
The elevator opens. Someone you know is already inside.

Both of you immediately look at your phones.
Both of you know the other person saw you.
Both are waiting for the other to speak first.

Doors open at the floor. In unison: "After you, after you."

This interaction consumed more energy than climbing ten flights of stairs.
en.editorNote: Send to any introvert — they will forward it to every introvert they know, in total silence
variations: Introvert's worst nightmare: the elevator is slow and stops on two extra floors. Social battery: zero.

items.sourceNote: 企鵝冷靜梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
為什麼企鵝永遠不緊張？

因為牠隨時都是「最酷」的那個。
不管對方說什麼，牠都能保持零度的表情。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個遇事永遠冷靜、臉不紅心不跳的朋友，讓他知道你在說他
variations: 企鵝的人生哲學：天氣越冷，我越自在——外界的壓力對我來說剛剛好。

en.content:
Why is a penguin never nervous?

Because it's always the coolest one in the room.
No matter what anyone says, it maintains zero-degree composure.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who stays perfectly calm in every crisis — they'll know you mean them
variations: Penguin life philosophy: the colder the environment, the more comfortable I am. Outside pressure suits me just fine.

items.sourceNote: 水餃諧音梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「你知道水餃為什麼永遠都準備好了嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為牠裡面什麼都包了，隨時都是『餡』（現）成的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 吃水餃或包餃子的時候說，旁邊的人通常會先沉默兩秒，然後說「你怎麼這樣」
variations: 湯圓版：湯圓為什麼這麼圓滿？因為它從出生就沒有稜角，凡事都能包容——這叫做先天優勢。

en.content:
"Do you know why dumplings are always ready to go?"
"Why?"
"Because they've got everything stuffed inside already.
They come fully loaded — there's nothing left to prepare."
en.editorNote: Say this while eating or folding dumplings — expect a two-second pause followed by 'why are you like this'
variations: Rice ball version: why is a mochi always so complete? Because it was born without sharp edges and can accommodate anything inside — that's called a natural advantage.

items.sourceNote: 棉被溫暖梗，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「你知道棉被為什麼這麼厲害嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為不管你今天過得多糟，它永遠張開手等你回來。
這叫做：無條件的『被（被愛）』。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 睡前傳給朋友，比任何晚安語錄都更讓人想鑽進棉被，而且說完笑完還是暖的
variations: 枕頭版：枕頭從來不評判你，不管你哭著還是笑著靠過去，它都接著——這也是一種無條件的支持。

en.content:
"Do you know what makes a blanket so great?"
"What?"
"No matter how terrible your day was, it's always there with open arms when you come back.
You could call it: unconditional cover-age."
en.editorNote: Send before sleep — more effective than any goodnight quote for making someone want to dive under the covers, and the warmth lasts past the pun
variations: Pillow version: a pillow never judges you — whether you come to it crying or laughing, it just accepts you. That's also a kind of unconditional support.

items.sourceNote: 大甲媽祖遶境健身梗，重新創作，限時（4/17–4/26）

zh-tw.content:
一位信眾跟著大甲媽祖遶境走了三天，回家打開計步器一看：
「距離本週健身目標只剩兩公里。」

信眾：「……下禮拜要報名健身房的錢可以省了。」

原來最有效的健康計畫，
不是月費制的，是有神明保佑的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 大甲媽祖遶境起駕期間（四月中旬）傳出去效果最好，跟沒跟去的人都能笑到——笑點在於，計步器說的是事實
variations: 延伸版：走完全程九天八夜的信眾，回家量體重發現數字沒變。結論：媽祖保佑的是心靈，體重的事還是要靠自己。

en.content:
A devotee followed the Mazu pilgrimage for three days, opened their step counter at home:
"You're only 2km away from your weekly fitness goal."

Devotee: "...I can cancel the gym membership I was about to sign up for."

Turns out the most effective fitness plan
isn't a monthly subscription — it's one with divine backing.
en.editorNote: Best shared during the Mazu pilgrimage season (mid-April) — works whether you participated or not, because the step counter is telling the truth either way
variations: Extended: a devotee completes all nine days, steps on the scale at home — the number hasn't changed. Conclusion: divine protection covers the spirit. The body is still your problem.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「what do you call a cold dog」經典冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
「冬天把小狗放在戶外，牠會變成什麼？」
「不知道，什麼？」
「熱狗。」
「……為什麼？」
「因為牠一直在發抖，根本沒辦法冷靜下來——還不是熱的？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 諧音加上荒謬邏輯的組合，適合在朋友說「說個笑話」的時候祭出，對方通常會先沉默再噗哧
variations: 冬天把貓咪放在戶外牠會變成什麼？還是貓咪——因為牠不管你說什麼，都不會理你的。

en.content:
"What do you call a dog left outside in winter?"
"I don't know, what?"
"A hot dog."
"...Why?"
"Because it won't stop shivering — clearly not a cool customer."
en.editorNote: Deadpan delivery is key — pause before the punchline for maximum groan
variations: What do you call a cat left outside in winter? Still a cat — it refuses to acknowledge the question.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自隱形墨水住院梗

zh-tw.content:
阿明不小心喝了一瓶隱形墨水。

家人趕快帶他去急診。

護理師問：「病患在哪裡？」

家人：「……這就是問題所在。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 說完要配上一臉嚴肅，讓對方先想一秒鐘，反應過來之後笑起來的那個時間差是精華
variations: 阿明喝了隱形墨水之後去照鏡子：鏡子裡什麼都沒有。他嚇了一跳——原來他也找不到自己了。

en.content:
Ben accidentally drank a bottle of invisible ink.

His family rushed him to the emergency room.

Nurse: "Where's the patient?"

Family: "...That's exactly the problem."
en.editorNote: Say it with a completely straight face — the time gap between delivery and reaction is where all the comedy lives
variations: Ben checked the mirror after drinking the invisible ink: nothing. He panicked — turns out even he couldn't find himself.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「someone glued my cards」撲克牌梗

zh-tw.content:
小傑回家發現有人把他整副撲克牌黏在一起了。

他站在那裡想了很久。

「這種事，我真的不知道該怎麼處理。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 說完停頓兩秒，讓對方反應「處理」這個雙關——撲克牌術語的「發牌/處理」跟「應對這件事」撞在一起
variations: 有人把他的魔術方塊每一面都塗成同一個顏色——他說：「這算是解開了嗎？我分不清楚。」

en.content:
Jake came home to find someone had glued his entire deck of cards together.

He stood there for a long moment.

"I honestly don't know how to deal with this."
en.editorNote: Pause after the punchline — let the listener catch the double meaning of 'deal' (handle the situation / deal cards) on their own
variations: Someone painted every side of his Rubik's Cube the same color. He stared at it: 'Is it solved? I genuinely cannot tell.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自長頸鹿被一眼認出的諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
「你知道長頸鹿為什麼永遠贏不了捉迷藏嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為牠一躲，馬上就被『看穿』了。」
「……是因為脖子太長嗎？」
「對，而且牠自己也知道，但就是改不了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合和小朋友玩的場合，大人小孩都懂，說完還可以接著問「你藏哪裡最好？」引發討論
variations: 斑馬也贏不了捉迷藏——因為牠走到哪裡都是最顯眼的那個，條紋藏不住的。

en.content:
"Why does a giraffe always lose at hide-and-seek?"
"Why?"
"Because no matter where it hides, it's always spotted."
"...Is that because of the neck?"
"The neck, the spots — everything. It knows. It just can't help it."
en.editorNote: Great for kids and adults alike — after the laugh, ask 'where would YOU hide?' to keep the conversation going
variations: A zebra has the same problem — wherever it goes, it's the most visible thing in the room. Stripes don't lie.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自月曆/日期冷笑話

zh-tw.content:
「你知道月曆為什麼這麼受歡迎嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為它的每一天都很『有日期』（有意義）。」

月曆：我每格都站著，哪天輪到我就認真過。

這種人生態度，很多人學不來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 年初換新月曆的時候說，或是年底回顧時說，兩個時機都能引發共鳴
variations: 月曆最悲傷的時刻：十二月底，只剩最後一頁，卻還是要笑著站在那裡等人撕掉它。

en.content:
"Why is a calendar so popular?"
"Why?"
"Because every single day, it shows up."

Calendar: I have a square for every day. When it's my turn, I give it everything.

That kind of dedication is hard to find.
en.editorNote: Say this when hanging a new calendar at New Year's, or when reflecting at year-end — both moments land perfectly
variations: The saddest moment for a calendar: late December, only one page left, still standing there waiting to be torn off with a smile.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自太空雙關梗「planet」

zh-tw.content:
「你知道太空人為什麼總是計畫很多事嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為他們從小就學會了要『星球（行星）』——每件事都要仔細計畫。」

太空人的人生信條：不先想清楚，就不敢出發。

畢竟在外太空，臨時改計畫的代價太高了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在討論計畫或職涯選擇的場合說，笑完之後還能引發認真討論
variations: 太空人最怕的不是隕石，是沒有計畫就出門的人——在太空裡，即興發揮不是風格，是事故。

en.content:
"Why do astronauts always plan everything so carefully?"
"Why?"
"Because they know you really have to planet."

Astronaut life rule: if you haven't thought it through, you don't launch.

Out in space, improvising isn't a style — it's an incident.
en.editorNote: Drop this when someone is overplanning something — the pun lands, then the conversation gets real
variations: The astronaut's greatest fear isn't meteors. It's someone who shows up unplanned. In space, going off-script isn't creativity — it's a crisis.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自雞舍四個門變轎車的雙關梗

zh-tw.content:
「你知道雞舍為什麼只能開兩扇門嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為如果開四扇，就變成轎車了。」

建築師：這個設計決定，我當初沒想這麼多。
雞：……我也沒想到我住在轎車裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 說完之後停頓兩秒，等對方繞回來——反應過來的那一刻是精華
variations: 狗屋如果有四個輪子算什麼？行動版豪宅——但狗不在乎，反正跟著主人走。

en.content:
"Why does a chicken coop only ever have two doors?"
"I don't know, why?"
"Because if it had four doors, it would be a chicken sedan."

Architect: I never considered this implication.
Chicken: I didn't either, but apparently I've been living in a coupe.
en.editorNote: Pause after the punchline — let them get the car/coupe connection on their own. The delayed reaction is the payoff.
variations: If a doghouse had four wheels, what would it be? A mobile estate. The dog doesn't care — it goes wherever you go anyway.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「no pun in ten did」雙關投稿梗

zh-tw.content:
小傑一口氣寫了十個諧音冷笑話投稿比賽。

他想：這麼多，至少一個能得獎吧。

結果一個都沒上。

評審留言：「十個裡沒有一個過關。」

小傑：「……這就是所謂的——十個冷笑話，一個都沒冷到人嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是投稿、老是落選的創作者朋友，他會先苦笑再說「這說的是我」
variations: 小傑第十一次投稿：只寫了一個，結果得了第一名。評審說：有時候，一個夠有力的，勝過十個平庸的。

en.content:
Jake submitted ten puns to a joke competition.

He figured with that many, surely at least one would win.

None of them placed.

The judges' note: "No pun in ten did."

Jake stared at the feedback for a long time.

Then he submitted it as his eleventh entry.
en.editorNote: Say 'no pun in ten did' out loud slowly — the 'intended' lands a second later. Classic delayed-reaction pun.
variations: Jake's twelfth submission: just one pun. It won first place. The judge said: one good one beats ten average ones every time.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「aware wolf」狼/環境意識雙關梗

zh-tw.content:
「你知道什麼樣的狼最危險嗎？」
「什麼樣的？」
「一隻非常有『環境意識』的狼。」
「為什麼？」
「因為牠對周遭的一切都超級敏感，你根本躲不過牠的注意——這種狼，叫做『超級覺察狼』。」
「……所以就是很機警的狼嗎？」
「對，但聽起來更厲害。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合跟喜歡環保或正念的朋友說，說完順便問他是不是也是『超級覺察型』
variations: 熊版本：一隻非常有自覺的熊，叫做『超自覺熊』——牠知道自己在幹嘛，但還是繼續幹。

en.content:
"What do you call a wolf that is extremely aware of its surroundings?"
"I don't know."
"A very aware wolf."
"...Is that it?"
"Say it faster."
"A very... awarewolf."
"There it is."
en.editorNote: Make them say it out loud — 'awarewolf' → 'a werewolf'. The moment they get it is the whole joke.
variations: Bear version: a bear with extremely high self-awareness is called a 'very self-aware bear.' It knows exactly what it's doing. It just keeps doing it anyway.

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「stayed up all night, then it dawned on me」日出雙關梗

zh-tw.content:
阿偉整晚沒睡，一直在思考一個問題：太陽到底去哪裡了？

就這樣想了一整夜。

天亮了。

阿偉：「啊——我突然『豁然開朗』了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 睡前說給熬夜族聽，或是傳給那個明明知道答案卻偏要想一整晚的朋友——說完大家一起笑，然後繼續熬夜
variations: 也可以換成：阿偉整晚在想為什麼月亮這麼圓。天亮了，他還是不知道，但他說：『沒關係，這件事讓我很充實。』

en.content:
Marcus stayed up all night trying to figure out where the sun goes after it sets.

He turned it over and over in his mind.

Then morning came.

And it dawned on him.
en.editorNote: Works best when someone announces they stayed up all night thinking about something — deliver the last line deadpan
variations: Marcus spent all night wondering why the moon is always so perfectly round. By morning he still had no answer, but he felt strangely fulfilled.

items.sourceNote: I人/E人 MBTI joke format; original writing; QuickSeek confirms I人/E人 top-3 trending Taiwan slang 2026

zh-tw.content:
I人的浪漫是：
想傳訊息給你，打了三行字，又全部刪掉，
最後只傳了一個問號。

那個問號，裝了很多想說的話。
zh-tw.editorNote: I人看了都說中，E人看了才理解為什麼I人這麼難猜。LINE群組傳這個，大家都會標出身邊的I人。
variations: 升級版：打了一整段，刪掉，換了個表情貼圖，最後還是沒傳出去。

en.content:
The romance of an introvert:
Thinking of texting you, typing three full sentences, deleting all of them,
then finally sending a single question mark.

That question mark carries a lot.
en.editorNote: Introverts will say 'this is so accurate.' Extroverts will finally understand why introverts are so hard to read. Great for tagging the I人 in your group chat.
variations: Advanced version: typed a whole paragraph, deleted it, switched to an emoji, then didn't send that either.

items.sourceNote: I人/E人 situational joke; original writing; E人 dragging I人 into social situations format

zh-tw.content:
E人讓I人最崩潰的一句話：

「剛好你也在，我順便幫你介紹一下大家！」

（I人內心OS：我沒有順便，我根本不想在。）
zh-tw.editorNote: I人看了會感到被理解，E人看了會反省一下。適合傳給那個每次見面都把你拉去認識一堆人的朋友。
variations: 更進階版：「你在旁邊站著幹嘛，來一起啦！」——I人的回答：我在充電。

en.content:
The most terrifying thing an extrovert can say to an introvert:

'Oh you're here too! Let me introduce you to everyone!'

(Introvert internal OS: I did not agree to this.)
en.editorNote: Introverts feel seen; extroverts might reconsider. Send to the friend who always pulls you into group introductions you never signed up for.
variations: Even worse: 'Why are you standing over there alone? Come join us!' — Introvert reply: I am recharging.

items.sourceNote: I人 elevator nightmare joke; original writing; classic social anxiety I人 scenario

zh-tw.content:
I人的電梯噩夢：

按了開門鍵，外面剛好站著一個認識的人。

兩個人一起進電梯，
接下來的十五秒是這輩子最漫長的沉默。

都在看手機。
都假裝對方不存在。
出門各走各的。
沒有人說再見。
zh-tw.editorNote: I人都有過這種經驗，看了又好笑又痛苦。E人看了完全不理解為什麼不直接聊天。
variations: 終極版：兩個人同一層，一起走到同一條走廊，都假裝要找東西，等對方先進門。

en.content:
The introvert's elevator nightmare:

You press the door-open button and someone you know is standing right there.

You both get in.
The next fifteen seconds are the longest silence of your life.

Both looking at phones.
Both pretending the other doesn't exist.
You exit on the same floor and go separate ways.
Neither of you says goodbye.
en.editorNote: Every introvert has lived this. Reading it is funny and painful at the same time. Extroverts who read it still don't understand why you didn't just talk.
variations: Ultimate version: same floor, same hallway — both pretend to look for something in your bag, waiting for the other to enter their room first.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳 2026新諧音梗; 眉莊=刪預算; viral wave confirmed by ETtoday and UDN Stars lazy pack April 2026

zh-tw.content:
宮廷裡最懂現代職場的角色：沈眉莊。

「眉莊」= 刪預算

每次看到她溫柔地勸說皇上，
我都覺得這是預算會議的既視感：
「皇上，這筆開銷還是省省吧……」
zh-tw.editorNote: 甄嬛傳諧音梗2026新浪潮，辦公室有預算壓力的人傳這個，秒懂。
variations: 進階版：每次主管說「我們砍一下預算」，我腦海裡自動出現沈眉莊的臉。

en.content:
The palace drama character who understands modern corporate life the most: Shen Meizhuang.

'Mei Zhuang' sounds like 'delete the budget' in Mandarin.

Every time she gently persuades the Emperor to cut spending,
it looks exactly like a budget review meeting:
'Your Majesty, perhaps we could economize on this...'
en.editorNote: 2026's viral 甄嬛傳 homophone wave. Anyone dealing with budget cuts at work will instantly relate.
variations: Every time my manager says 'let's trim the budget,' Shen Meizhuang's face automatically appears.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳諧音梗 壯世代=三阿哥長高了/銀髮政策; viral per UDN Stars lazy pack 2026

zh-tw.content:
甄嬛傳裡，齊妃每次出場必說：
「三阿哥又長高了！」

網友發現：
三阿哥長高了 = 壯世代

所以齊妃其實是在推動銀髮政策，
她只是早了幾百年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 甄嬛傳新梗，搭配2026年台灣「壯世代」政策話題更好笑。適合轉給喜歡古裝劇也懂時事的朋友。
variations: 延伸：齊妃每次出場就說一次壯世代，她才是宮廷裡最有前瞻力的人。

en.content:
In the palace drama, Concubine Qi has one signature line every scene:
'The third prince has grown so tall!'

Netizens figured it out:
'Third prince grown tall' sounds like 'strong generation' in Mandarin — Taiwan's active aging policy.

So she was advocating for the silver economy all along.
Just a few centuries too early.
en.editorNote: 2026 viral 甄嬛傳 pun. Even better with context about Taiwan's '壯世代' policy. Send to drama fans who also follow the news.
variations: Every time Concubine Qi appears to say 'strong generation,' she's the most forward-thinking person in the palace.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳諧音梗 舌年行大運=靜白割舌/蛇年; 2026蛇年新年梗 per UDN Stars

zh-tw.content:
2026年是蛇年，
而甄嬛傳早就給了今年最應景的賀詞：

靜白那幕 = 舌年行大運

祝你2026蛇年大運。
（來源有保證，請放心轉傳。）
zh-tw.editorNote: 甄嬛傳諧音梗新浪潮，舌年梗適合年初還是現在傳都有效。配上劇照更好笑，適合傳給懂梗的朋友。
variations: 「舌」年行大運，比普通蛇年賀詞有創意多了，而且出典經典。

en.content:
2026 is the Year of the Snake —
and the palace drama already had the perfect New Year greeting ready:

A certain scene with Jing Bai:
= 'Tongue year, great fortune' — sounds like 'Snake year, great fortune' in Mandarin.

Happy 2026 Year of the Snake.
(Certified dramatic source. Safe to forward.)
en.editorNote: 2026 viral 甄嬛傳 homophone — still relevant well past the new year. Best sent with a drama still to friends who know the reference.
variations: 'Tongue year, great fortune' is far more creative than a standard Snake Year greeting — with certified iconic origins.

items.sourceNote: 大甲媽祖遶境 April 17-26 2026; 60萬人參與; 沿路小吃文化 humor angle

zh-tw.content:
大甲媽祖遶境9天8夜結束後，
有人打開計步器，發現：

走完全程，帳面上達到了半年的健身目標。

但沿路的糖葫蘆、鹽酥雞、芋圓……
剛好也抵消掉了。

信仰與卡路里，完美平衡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 遶境文化中，信仰和沿途小吃是並行的兩個主題。這個笑話輕鬆致敬了兩者。適合傳給有參加或關注今年遶境的朋友（4/17-4/26）。
variations: 遶境前：「我去是為了信仰。」遶境後：「信仰和鹽酥雞，我兩個都要。」

en.content:
After nine days following the Mazu pilgrimage,
someone checked their step counter and discovered:

they'd hit six months' worth of fitness goals in one week.

The candied hawthorn, fried chicken, and taro balls along the route, though...
canceled it out perfectly.

Faith and calories: perfectly balanced.
en.editorNote: The Dajia Mazu Pilgrimage (April 17–26) is Taiwan's largest annual religious procession, with 600,000 participants. Street food stalls line the entire route — this joke celebrates both devotion and snacking.
variations: Before: 'I'm going for faith.' After: 'Faith and fried chicken. I want both.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自月曆諧音冷笑話系列；corner / 90 degrees 溫度梗重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「天氣這麼冷，你家有沒有什麼取暖的好辦法？」
「有啊，站到牆角去。」
「牆角？那有什麼用？」
「因為牆角永遠是九十度。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 說完停兩秒讓對方算一下，反應過來的那一刻才是笑點——適合冬天或在冷氣房裡說
variations: 升級版：「兩個牆角靠在一起是幾度？」「一百八十度。」「那你家有幾個牆角？」「四個。」「恭喜，你家加起來三百六十度，等於一個完整的圓，一點都不冷。」

en.content:
"It's freezing in here. Any tips for warming up?"
"Sure — go stand in the corner."
"The corner? How does that help?"
"Because a corner is always ninety degrees."
en.editorNote: Pause after the punchline — let them do the geometry for a second. The delayed realization is where the laugh lives. Works any time someone complains about the cold.
variations: Follow-up: 'Two corners make 180 degrees. Four corners make 360 — a full circle. Your whole room is technically on fire mathematically.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「you can tuna piano」音樂諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
「你可以幫鋼琴調音，但你可以調一條魚嗎？」
「……什麼意思？」
「調音（tuning）和鮪魚（tuna）——鋼琴可以tune，鮪魚不行。」
「這也太冷了。」
「謝謝，我練了很久。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 英文雙關梗改寫成中文版，台灣愛吃生魚片的人聽了特別有感。說完要補一句「謝謝」，效果翻倍
variations: 延伸版：鮪魚雖然不能被調音，但牠可以被切成薄片——這叫做用另一種方式被人欣賞。

en.content:
"You can tune a piano, but you can't tuna fish."
"...That's it?"
"That's it."
"That's awful."
"I know. I've been working on it for years."
en.editorNote: Say it with complete sincerity — the worse you feel about telling it, the better it lands. A classic that never stops being terrible.
variations: Follow-up: 'You also can't piano a tuna. I tried. The tuna was not appreciative.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「key witness」鎖匠法庭雙關梗

zh-tw.content:
「你聽說了嗎？隔壁那個配鑰匙的師傅上法院當證人。」
「為什麼？」
「因為他是這個案子裡最關鍵的人——他是唯一的『鑰匙』人（關鍵人）。」

法官：請問你的職業？
配鑰匙師傅：我做鑰匙的。
法官：那你是不是這個案子的關鍵人物？
配鑰匙師傅：……您是第一個這樣問的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「關鍵」和「鑰匙」的諧音梗，配上情境感更強。說完要一臉認真，讓對方先想一下
variations: 配鑰匙師傅最後說：「我每天就是在幫人解決問題——但這次的問題是別人幫我解決的，我只是來說明的。」

en.content:
"Did you hear about the locksmith who had to testify in court?"
"What happened?"
"He was the key witness."

Judge: What is your occupation?
Locksmith: I make keys.
Judge: So you're the key figure in this case?
Locksmith: ...You're the first person who's ever asked me that.
en.editorNote: Deadpan delivery is essential — let the 'key witness / key figure' land without telegraphing it. The locksmith's final line is optional, for when you want to stretch the bit.
variations: The locksmith afterwards: 'I spend my days solving people's problems. Today, it was someone else's problem — I was just there to explain things.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自音樂老師 sharp notes / treble 雙關梗，重新發展成情境笑話

zh-tw.content:
音樂老師問學生：「你今天為什麼遲到？」
學生：「因為我在練習把拍子打準。」
老師：「你練了多久？」
學生：「一個小時。」
老師：「那你還是遲到了。」
學生：「對，但至少我遲到得很有節奏感。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給喜歡音樂或學過樂器的朋友，說完讓對方判斷學生到底有沒有道理——笑話完了還可以討論
variations: 延伸版：音樂老師說：「下次再遲到，我就請你出去。」學生說：「那我可以選自己的出場曲嗎？」

en.content:
Music teacher: "Why were you late today?"
Student: "I was practicing my timing."
Teacher: "For how long?"
Student: "About an hour."
Teacher: "And you're still late."
Student: "Yes, but I was late with perfect rhythm."
en.editorNote: Great for anyone who's studied an instrument — ask them afterward if the student has a point. The joke opens into a conversation.
variations: Extended: Teacher says 'Late again and I'll ask you to leave.' Student: 'Can I at least pick my exit music?'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自風力發電機 metal fan 雙關梗，改成台灣電風扇情境重新發展

zh-tw.content:
兩台電風扇站在廣場上聊天。
其中一台問：「你平常喜歡聽什麼音樂？」
另一台說：「我超迷金屬樂的。」
第一台：「難怪，你本來就是金屬做的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給喜歡音樂或工程類的朋友，冷到讓人翻白眼
variations: 延伸版：第一台問：「那你喜歡哪個樂團？」第二台：「Iron Maiden 啊，我的偶像。」

en.content:
Two electric fans are chatting in the park.
One asks, "What kind of music are you into?"
The other says, "I'm a huge metal fan."
First fan: "Of course you are. You're literally made of metal."
en.editorNote: Send this to your music-loving or engineer friends — the groan is guaranteed
variations: Extended: 'Which band?' 'Iron Maiden, obviously. Role models.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「把錢放冰箱是為了冷現金」雙關梗，改成台灣家庭情境

zh-tw.content:
小美問爸爸：「你為什麼把紅包放進冰箱？」
爸爸：「因為我想要『冷』靜地存錢。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 過年期間傳給長輩或親友，喜氣洋洋又帶點冷
variations: 延伸版：媽媽說：「那你的理財計畫就是讓錢冷卻？」爸爸：「對，這樣才不會衝動消費。」

en.content:
"Dad, why did you put your wallet in the freezer?"
"Because I want to keep my money cool, calm, and collected."
"That's not how savings accounts work."
"But it's how I work."
en.editorNote: Perfect for anyone who jokes about their terrible spending habits — the punchline lands best when delivered deadpan
variations: Short version: 'Why is your cash in the freezer?' 'Cold hard cash, obviously.'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「天冷站牆角因為有90度」幾何雙關梗，改成學校考試情境

zh-tw.content:
數學老師問：「天氣這麼冷，你知道哪裡最暖和嗎？」
學生想了想：「牆角？」
老師：「答對了！因為牆角是九十度。」
學生：「……老師你的考卷也是這種題目嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合數學老師或理工系朋友，講完可以問對方：「這是冷笑話還是幾何題？」
variations: 簡短版：「冬天哪裡最暖？牆角，因為九十度嘛！」

en.content:
Math teacher: "It's freezing outside. Where's the warmest spot in the room?"
Student: "The corner?"
Teacher: "Exactly — it's always 90 degrees there."
Student: "Is this a math class or a stand-up show?"
en.editorNote: Great for geometry teachers or STEM friends — works best said with a completely straight face
variations: Short version: 'Why is the corner always warm? Because it's 90 degrees!'

items.sourceNote: 改寫自「撲克牌被黏住不知道如何deal」雙關梗，改成桌遊聚會情境

zh-tw.content:
小傑打開桌遊箱發現牌都黏在一起了。
他沉默了一下說：「我……不知道該怎麼處理這件事。」
朋友：「你真的不知道怎麼『發牌』嗎？」
小傑：「對，我的人生也是這樣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 桌遊聚會或朋友聚餐時講，接著可以說「好啦我們改玩別的」，無縫轉場
variations: 延伸版：「算了，換我來洗牌好了。」「那也得先把牌分開吧？」「這就是人生的第一步。」

en.content:
Jake opened the card box and found every card was stuck together.
He stared for a moment and said, "I honestly don't know how to deal with this."
His friend said, "Bro, that's literally your job right now."
Jake: "Story of my life."
en.editorNote: Perfect for game nights — the pun works on two levels and the existential follow-up gets extra laughs
variations: Short version: 'Someone glued my cards together. I have no idea how to deal with it.'

items.sourceNote: I人/E人 MBTI joke format; original content

zh-tw.content:
E人讓I人最崩潰的事：

路上剛好遇到認識的人，E人一臉興奮：
「哦！他也是我朋友，我介紹你們認識！」

I人（內心）：請別。系統當機。請求拒絕。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給你身邊那個超會社交的E人，讓他知道他的熱情有時候讓I人需要重啟
variations: E人：你們一定會很合得來！I人（已經在想要怎麼結束這段對話）：嗯……好。

en.content:
The most overwhelming thing E types do to I types:

Running into someone on the street, the E immediately brightens:
'Oh! They're my friend too, you HAVE to meet them!'

Introvert internally: Error. System overload. Request denied.
en.editorNote: Share with your most social extrovert friend — let them know their enthusiasm has real consequences
variations: E type: 'You two will get along so well!' I type (already planning the exit): '...sure.'

items.sourceNote: I人電梯噩夢 joke format; original content

zh-tw.content:
I人的電梯噩夢：

進去之後發現裡面站著一個認識的人。
兩個人都低頭看手機。
電梯停在每一層。

每一秒都是考驗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個辦公室的I人同事，讓他知道你懂他的痛苦
variations: I人看到認識的人走向同一部電梯，會毫不猶豫地走向樓梯。即使要爬十二層。

en.content:
The introvert's elevator nightmare:

You step in and realize someone you vaguely know is already there.
Both of you immediately look at your phones.
The elevator stops at every floor.

Every second is a trial.
en.editorNote: Send to the quiet colleague who takes the stairs specifically to avoid small talk
variations: An introvert will unhesitatingly walk toward the stairs the moment they spot an acquaintance heading for the same elevator. Twelve floors? Fine.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳2026病毒式諧音梗，眉預算角度，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
2026最新宮廷諧音梗：

沈眉莊每次替人說情都沒有下文——
網友：這不就是「沒預算」？

怪不得皇上每次都推說「需要再研究研究」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給最近在公司被砍預算的朋友，讓他在苦笑中找到歷史共鳴
variations: 沈眉莊：我替你說了很多好話。結果：預算還是被砍了。這叫「眉預算」。

en.content:
2026's hottest palace drama wordplay:

Every time Lady Shen advocates for someone at court, nothing comes of it.
The internet's take: 'She's basically the Budget Department.'

No wonder the Emperor always says 'we'll study it further.'
en.editorNote: Send to a coworker whose project budget just got cut — shared suffering through historical fiction
variations: Lady Shen: I spoke up for you extensively. Result: budget still cut. This is the 'Budget Shen' effect.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳2026病毒式諧音梗，壯世代角度，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
甄嬛傳2026新角度：
齊妃每次見皇上，都驕傲地說：「三阿哥又長高了！」

網友：這難道不就是「壯世代」？

連宮廷劇都在呼應人口政策，厲害。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給對社會時事有關注的朋友，諧音梗跟時事一起笑，有層次
variations: 齊妃代言詞：三阿哥越來越壯，就是壯世代的最佳代表。沒有人要求她說，但她說了。

en.content:
A fresh 2026 take on the palace drama:
Lady Qi greets the Emperor every episode with: 'The Third Prince has grown so strong again!'

The internet's take: 'She's literally the spokesperson for robust demographic growth.'

Even period dramas are doing population policy commentary now.
en.editorNote: Share with friends who follow social commentary — the joke works on two layers for anyone who caught the meme
variations: Lady Qi, unasked: 'The Third Prince represents peak vitality.' Nobody commissioned this statement. She delivered it anyway.

items.sourceNote: 甄嬛傳2026病毒式諧音梗，慣蘭高手角度，重新改寫

zh-tw.content:
甄嬛傳的職場心得：

華妃把所有不守規矩的人都管得服服帖帖，
有那麼多應對慣老闆的方法，
難道不就是「慣蘭高手」？

后宮生存指南，現代通用。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給在公司最了解如何應對慣老闆的那個同事，精準有共鳴
variations: 面對慣老闆，跟面對華妃是一樣的——表面恭敬，內心早就想好了下一步。

en.content:
Workplace lessons from the inner palace:

Lady Hua has mastered every technique for managing difficult, entitled people.
She's basically the ultimate expert in handling demanding bosses.

Inner palace survival guide: modern edition.
en.editorNote: Send to the colleague who's best at navigating the office's most difficult manager — they'll feel very seen
variations: Dealing with an entitled boss and dealing with Lady Hua require the same skill: outward deference, inward strategy.

items.sourceNote: 大甲媽祖遶境2026時事，輕鬆角度，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
跟著媽祖走9天8夜340公里，
回來打開計步器，
才終於看到一個讓你想截圖分享的數字。

原來信仰，也是最好的健身打卡動力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 大甲媽祖遶境期間（4月中至4月底）分享，台灣人都能會心一笑；平時也適合傳給那個一直說「我最近在運動」的朋友
variations: 健身目標達成方法有很多種，其中一種是跟著媽祖走9天。效果保證，但過程很考驗信仰。

en.content:
After walking 340 kilometers in 9 days following a religious procession,
you check your step tracker —
and for the very first time, you actually want to screenshot the number.

Turns out, faith is one of the best fitness motivators out there.
en.editorNote: Works perfectly during Taiwan's Mazu Pilgrimage season (mid to late April) — also good for sending to that friend who's been 'kind of exercising lately'
variations: There are many ways to hit your fitness goals. Following the Mazu procession for nine days is one of them. Results guaranteed. Faith tested.

items.sourceNote: 受台灣冷笑話集錦啟發，諧音梗重新創作

zh-tw.content:
問：蝸牛爬上樹幹，要花多久時間？

答：很久啊，因為牠每走一步都要「殼」一下。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個凡事慢吞吞、但自己還很有理的朋友
variations: 延伸版：蝸牛為什麼不趕路？因為牠覺得「慢工出細殼」。

en.content:
Q: How long does it take a snail to climb a tree?

A: A very long time — it has to stop at every step to "shell" things over.
en.editorNote: Perfect for that friend who takes forever to make a decision but always has a reason
variations: Bonus: Why doesn't a snail ever rush? Because it believes in taking things one shell at a time.

items.sourceNote: 受台灣冷笑話集錦啟發，職場梗重新創作

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「你今天怎麼這麼準時？」

我說：「因為我夢到主管，嚇醒的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給還在熬夜趕報告的同事，秒懂不用解釋
variations: 延伸版：有人問如何讓自己起得來？設三個鬧鐘：一個是鬧鐘，一個是備用，一個是主管的傳訊通知音。

en.content:
Coworker: "Wow, you're actually on time today. What happened?"

Me: "I had a dream about my boss. Woke up in a cold sweat."
en.editorNote: Send to the coworker who's been pulling all-nighters — they'll feel very understood
variations: Variation: How do you make sure you wake up on time? Three alarms: one regular, one backup, and one set to your boss's notification sound.

items.sourceNote: 受英文冷笑話諧音梗啟發，雙語創作，天氣主題

zh-tw.content:
問：為什麼下雨天特別適合工作？

答：因為那是唯一一個，你說「我有點low（鬱悶）」，大家都覺得很正常的日子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在下雨的週一早上傳給群組，精準捕捉集體心情
variations: 下雨天人為什麼效率低？因為心情也跟著「潮濕」了。

en.content:
Q: Why is a rainy day the best day to get work done?

A: It's the one day you can say "I'm feeling a little under the weather" and no one questions your productivity.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat on a grey Monday morning — universal mood
variations: Why is productivity low on rainy days? Simple: the atmosphere is just too *draining*.

items.sourceNote: 受台灣冷笑話靠北梗啟發，食物諧音梗重新創作

zh-tw.content:
問：香蕉皮為什麼很有智慧？

答：因為它看過太多人「跌跌撞撞」，早就看透人生了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個自認為看透一切、最愛說「我早就知道了」的朋友
variations: 延伸版：香蕉為什麼不說話？因為話說多了，就只剩一身「皮」了。

en.content:
Q: Why is a banana peel so wise?

A: It's watched countless people fall flat on their faces. At this point, it's basically a life coach.
en.editorNote: Send to your friend who always says "I told you so" — they'll either laugh or feel called out
variations: Bonus: Why don't bananas give advice? Because every time they open up, someone slips.

items.sourceNote: 受Dcard諧音梗討論串啟發，科技生活梗重新創作

zh-tw.content:
手機電量剩1%，
你還是猶豫要不要充電。

這不就是現代人的縮影嗎——
明明快撐不住了，還在問「再撐一下可以嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個平常硬撐不休息、手機電量跟精神狀態一樣低的朋友
variations: 延伸版：手機沒電會自動關機，人沒電卻還被要求繼續開會。科技始終來自人性，但人性從來不給自己充電時間。

en.content:
Your phone is at 1% battery.
You're still wondering if you can squeeze out one more scroll.

This is modern life in a nutshell:
almost dead, but still asking "just five more minutes?"
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who runs on no sleep and a dying phone — they'll laugh, then immediately feel attacked
variations: Phones shut down automatically at 0%. People don't get that feature. We just keep going until someone finally says "you need to rest."

items.sourceNote: 受英文dad joke競賽諧音梗啟發，pun in ten did改寫為台式雙語版本

zh-tw.content:
我投了十個笑話去比賽，
心想數量多，至少一個能得獎。

結果評審說：「十個都沒過。」

我驚呆了——這不就是「十投十敗」嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給正在參加各種比賽或申請屢戰屢敗的朋友，笑中帶淚
variations: 延伸版：後來我只投了一個，結果得了第一。評審說：「這次終於只有一個爛笑話了，比較好選。」

en.content:
I entered ten jokes into a pun contest, convinced that with ten shots, at least one would land.

Not a single one placed.

I guess no pun in ten did.
en.editorNote: Great for anyone who's been sending out applications or pitches with no luck — groan-worthy but relatable
variations: Bonus: I tried submitting just one pun the next time. It won. Turns out quality beats quantity — even in bad jokes.

items.sourceNote: 受台灣漫漫日和諧音笑話集啟發，肩膀shoulder燒的梗改寫成對話版

zh-tw.content:
天氣冷，朋友說：「好冷喔，怎麼辦？」

我說：「摸我肩膀。」

他困惑：「為什麼？」

我說：「因為肩膀是shoulder，『燒的』啊！」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在冬天群組傳這個，尤其是跟懂台語的朋友聊天時特別有梗
variations: 延伸版：「那腳呢？」「腳是足，足夠熱了。」

en.content:
Friend: "It's freezing in here. What should I do?"

Me: "Touch my shoulder."

Friend: "Why??"

Me: "Because the shoulder is always the warmest part of the body — it's literally a *boulder* of warmth."
en.editorNote: Best delivered in person with a completely straight face for maximum groan reaction
variations: Alternate punchline: "Because you should *shoulder* the cold — it builds character."

items.sourceNote: 受beaver dam joke啟發，改寫為台式動物諧音梗，用烏龜苦瓜(call龜)概念重組

zh-tw.content:
問：打電話給烏龜，猜一種蔬菜？

答：苦瓜。

因為打電話就是「call」，call龜……苦瓜！
zh-tw.editorNote: 腦筋急轉彎風格，適合傳給喜歡猜謎的朋友，講完記得等對方想三秒再公布答案
variations: 延伸版：打電話給南瓜，猜一個動作？答案：叫南（call南），召喚！

en.content:
Q: I watched a documentary about beavers last night.

A: Honestly? Best dam show I've ever seen.
en.editorNote: Classic dad joke format — works best when said totally deadpan, then watch the slow-dawning groan
variations: Variation: I tried to watch a show about dams afterward. It was just too *riveting*.

items.sourceNote: 受locksmith key witness joke啟發，改寫為台式職業諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
為什麼鎖匠出庭當證人，法官特別重視他的證詞？

因為他掌握了整件事的「關鍵」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在需要暖場的會議開頭講，尤其對法律或商業背景的人有雙重效果
variations: 延伸版：後來鎖匠辭職改行當記者，採訪風格還是老樣子——每次都直接切入「核心」。

en.content:
A locksmith was called to testify in court last week.

Makes sense — he was the *key* witness.
en.editorNote: Deadpan delivery is everything here. Say it like it's totally serious, then watch the room react
variations: Follow-up: The judge said his testimony really *unlocked* the whole case.

items.sourceNote: 受seagull/bay/bagel英文dad joke啟發，改寫為台式地理諧音版本

zh-tw.content:
問：老鷹為什麼不飛去基隆港？

答：因為牠一到基隆就變成「雞」隆了，沒面子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合跟喜歡台灣地名諧音的朋友傳，說完要若無其事等對方慢慢反應
variations: 延伸版：老鷹後來改飛花蓮——因為那裡讓牠感覺「花」樣年「鷹」。

en.content:
Q: Why don't seagulls ever fly over the bay?

A: Because then they'd be bagels — and nobody wants to be a breakfast item.
en.editorNote: Classic groaner — best told deadpan at a brunch table and watch someone spit out their coffee
variations: Follow-up: The seagulls tried flying over the lake instead. Now they're just *lake-els*. Still not great.

items.sourceNote: 受stairs/up to something英文dad joke啟發，改寫為台式電梯樓梯版本

zh-tw.content:
我從來不信任電梯。

牠每次看起來都很可疑——
老是在「上下其手」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給辦公室朋友，尤其是那種被主管搞得霧煞煞的日子，笑完順便抱怨一下
variations: 延伸版：樓梯更糟，每次都在「算計」你——你不上它，它就讓你喘不過氣。

en.content:
I never trust elevators.

There's just something about them — they're always *up to something*.

And don't even get me started on escalators. Those things are constantly *stepping it up*.
en.editorNote: Deadpan delivery is key — say it like a genuine safety concern, then wait for the groan
variations: Alternate version: I told my friend I don't trust stairs either. He said I need to take it one step at a time.

items.sourceNote: 受facial hair grew on me英文dad joke啟發，改寫為台式外表轉變自嘲梗

zh-tw.content:
我以前很討厭自己的大肚子。

但後來想想……

它陪我走過了這麼多年，
感情已經「長」在身上了，捨不得了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個嘴上說要減肥、實際上繼續大吃的朋友，搭配一個無奈的表情貼圖效果更佳
variations: 延伸版：朋友說你的肚子是「世界遺產」——失去了就沒了，要好好保護。

en.content:
I used to absolutely hate my beard.

But honestly? It really grew on me.

Much like my gut, my bad habits, and my collection of dad jokes.
en.editorNote: Great self-deprecating opener — works especially well if you actually have a beard or a gut, or both
variations: Variation: I tried shaving it off once. Turns out my face had forgotten what it looked like underneath. We're both moving on.

items.sourceNote: 受elevator works on many levels英文dad joke啟發，改寫為台式職場多層次諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
我老闆說我是個「多層次」的員工。

我一開始以為是誇我。

後來發現他的意思是：
我一個人身兼多職，薪水卻只有一層。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給同樣身兼多職、被壓榨的職場朋友，苦笑一下繼續撐
variations: 延伸版：老闆後來說我是「全方位人才」，我說謝謝，然後悄悄更新了履歷。

en.content:
My boss told me my work operates on many levels.

I said, "Is that a compliment?"

He said, "Not really — you've just got too many jobs and not enough pay to match any of them."
en.editorNote: Office humor with a sting — best shared with overworked coworkers over coffee before Monday morning meetings
variations: Alternate punchline: I only make jokes about elevators. They work on many levels. Unlike my career trajectory.

items.sourceNote: 受seafood diet / see food英文dad joke啟發，改寫為台式吃貨飲食諧音版本

zh-tw.content:
朋友問：「你在吃什麼飲食法？」

我說：「我在吃『看食法』。」

「什麼是看食法？」

「就是看到什麼，就吃什麼。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給正在崩潰減肥計劃的朋友，或是放棄節食後的自我安慰
variations: 延伸版：後來我改良了一下——叫做「看到才不吃法」，結果眼睛閉起來吃更多了。

en.content:
Friend: "Are you on any kind of diet?"

Me: "Yeah, I'm on a seafood diet."

Friend: "Oh, like fish and vegetables?"

Me: "No. I *see* food, and I eat it."
en.editorNote: Timeless groaner — deliver it completely straight, as if you're genuinely proud of your dietary philosophy
variations: Follow-up: I tried the see-food diet at a buffet last weekend. I blacked out after the third plate. No regrets.

items.sourceNote: 受stacked pancakes父親節梗啟發，改寫為台式早餐店場景諧音版

zh-tw.content:
兒子問爸爸：「為什麼你早餐都點鬆餅？」

爸爸說：「因為人生已經夠『硬』了，至少早餐要『鬆』一下。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在週末早餐桌上，爸爸自帶得意笑容講給全家聽
variations: 延伸版：媽媽接話：「那我點吐司，因為我已經被你『土』到不行了。」

en.content:
Son: "Dad, why do you always order pancakes for breakfast?"

Dad: "Because life is already stacked against me — might as well start the day with something stacked for me."
en.editorNote: Classic dad delivery — pause before the punchline and look proudly at your stack
variations: Alt: Son: 'Why pancakes again?' Dad: 'Because waffles are too edgy for a Sunday.'

items.sourceNote: 受math book has too many problems英文雙關啟發，改寫為台式學生考試吐槽版

zh-tw.content:
同學問我：「你為什麼最討厭數學課本？」

我說：「因為它『題』目太多，我『提』不起勁。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合考試週傳給同樣崩潰的同學，互相苦中作樂
variations: 延伸版：後來我換了英文課本，結果它『單字』太多，我『單身』也太久。

en.content:
Classmate: "Why do you hate your math textbook so much?"

Me: "Because it's got too many problems — and frankly, so do I. We're not compatible."
en.editorNote: Perfect for sharing during finals week — relatable groan for any student
variations: Alt punchline: "I tried to break up with it, but it kept saying 'let x equal us'."

items.sourceNote: 受year of the bark新年諧音梗啟發，改寫為台式養寵物雙關版

zh-tw.content:
朋友問：「你家狗叫什麼名字？」

我說：「牠叫『旺來』。」

「為什麼？」

「因為牠一叫，鳳梨酥就不見了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合過年期間傳給有養寵物的朋友，順便配一張寵物偷吃零食的照片
variations: 延伸版：我後來又養了一隻貓，取名叫『發財』。結果牠只負責『發』脾氣，財沒看到。

en.content:
Friend: "What's your dog's name?"

Me: "I named him 2026."

Friend: "Why?"

Me: "Because every year with him is the year of the bark."
en.editorNote: Great opener at the dog park — works year-round, but extra punchy in January
variations: Alt: "I also have a cat named Resolution. She never lasts past February."

items.sourceNote: 受pencil joke the point kept changing啟發，改寫為台式辦公室文具諧音梗

zh-tw.content:
同事問：「你最近在寫什麼？」

我說：「我在寫一篇關於鉛筆的文章。」

「寫得怎樣？」

「沒重點。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳到辦公室群組打破週一會議前的沉默
variations: 延伸版：後來我改寫橡皮擦，結果寫到一半，自己擦掉了。

en.content:
Coworker: "What are you writing about?"

Me: "An article about pencils."

Coworker: "How's it going?"

Me: "Honestly? Pointless."
en.editorNote: Slack-channel material — drop it right before the Monday standup to soften the mood
variations: Alt: "I switched to writing about erasers. Halfway through, I changed my mind and removed everything."

items.sourceNote: 受Wi-Fi joke didn't connect啟發，改寫為台式遠距上班雙關版

zh-tw.content:
我老婆說：「你最近講話越來越冷。」

我說：「沒辦法，我家Wi-Fi訊號太弱。」

「跟Wi-Fi有什麼關係？」

「我講的笑話，連我家路由器都收不到。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合丟在遠距工作的同事群組，配一張自家Wi-Fi訊號圖更有梗
variations: 延伸版：後來我換了Mesh，結果笑話還是傳不出去，工程師說那是內容問題。

en.content:
Wife: "Your jokes have been getting colder lately."

Me: "It's the Wi-Fi."

Wife: "What does Wi-Fi have to do with it?"

Me: "Even my router refuses to connect to them."
en.editorNote: Drop this in the remote-team Slack right after someone complains about lag
variations: Alt: "I upgraded to mesh Wi-Fi. Now my jokes have full bars and still won't land."

items.sourceNote: 受noble gases no one reacted啟發，改寫為台式化學課堂諧音版

zh-tw.content:
化學老師問：「為什麼氦氣最沒朋友？」

全班沉默。

老師：「因為它『氦』羞，講話都沒人理。」

台下傳來一句：「老師，我們是沒反應，不是沒朋友。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合理組同學上化學課傳紙條，或考前群組鬆一下
variations: 延伸版：後來老師改講氧氣的笑話，全班終於有反應了——因為大家都需要呼吸。

en.content:
Chemistry teacher: "Why does helium have no friends?"

Class: silence.

Teacher: "Because nobody reacts to him."

Student in the back: "Sir, that's us, not the helium."
en.editorNote: Perfect for a group chat full of STEM majors the night before midterms
variations: Alt: "Then she tried an oxygen joke. The class finally reacted — turns out we needed it to breathe."

items.sourceNote: 受smart fridge cold shoulder啟發，改寫為台式智慧家電擬人雙關版

zh-tw.content:
我跟智慧冰箱吵架了。

我老婆問：「你們在吵什麼？」

我說：「牠一直給我冷臉。」

老婆：「牠是冰箱欸。」

我：「但我講話牠也都不回，這就過分了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合家庭群組，配一張冰箱貼滿便利貼的照片更傳神
variations: 延伸版：後來我跟掃地機器人和好了，因為至少牠會主動繞著我轉。

en.content:
I got into a fight with my smart fridge.

Wife: "What are you two arguing about?"

Me: "It keeps giving me the cold shoulder."

Wife: "It's a fridge."

Me: "Yeah, but it also left me on read. That's the part that hurts."
en.editorNote: Solid Father's Day card material — pairs well with a photo of an overstuffed fridge
variations: Alt: "I made up with the robot vacuum instead. At least it circles back to me."

items.sourceNote: 受coffee got mugged啟發，改寫為台式手搖飲報案諧音版

zh-tw.content:
我去警察局報案。

警察問：「先生，發生什麼事？」

我說：「我的珍奶被搶了。」

警察：「歹徒長怎樣？」

我：「他『吸』走了所有珍珠。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合排隊買手搖時跟朋友講，破冰指數五顆星
variations: 延伸版：警察問還有沒有線索，我說：「現場只剩一個空杯，跟我同樣空虛的心。」

en.content:
I went to the police station to file a report.

Officer: "Sir, what happened?"

Me: "My boba got mugged."

Officer: "Can you describe the suspect?"

Me: "He sucked up every last pearl."
en.editorNote: Use this while waiting in a long boba line — guaranteed groan from the friend behind you
variations: Alt: "The only evidence left was an empty cup. And, you know, my dignity."

items.sourceNote: 受『電腦感冒因為視窗沒關』啟發，改寫為台式辦公室冷氣版

zh-tw.content:
同事問我：「你的筆電怎麼一直在咳嗽？」

我說：「因為它感冒了。」

同事：「電腦怎麼會感冒？」

我：「因為我沒關視窗，冷氣一直灌進去。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在辦公室冷氣超強的時候講，搭配假裝咳嗽更冷
variations: 延伸版：同事問怎麼治療，我說：『重開機，再多喝熱水。』

en.content:
My coworker asked: "Why does your laptop keep coughing?"

Me: "It caught a cold."

Coworker: "How does a computer catch a cold?"

Me: "I left all my Windows open."
en.editorNote: Best delivered in an over-air-conditioned office, ideally while shivering on purpose
variations: Follow up: "The fix? Reboot and lots of hot tea."

items.sourceNote: 受『雪人冷肩膀』one-liner啟發，改寫為對話式情境版

zh-tw.content:
我跟雪人吵架。

朋友問：「你們吵什麼？」

我說：「他每次都不理我。」

朋友：「那是當然，他本來就是冷漠的人。」

我：「對啊，整個人都是冰塊做的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在寒流來襲的時候講，越冷越好笑
variations: 也可以改成跟冰箱吵架：「他連門都不肯為我開。」

en.content:
I had a fight with a snowman.

Friend: "What were you fighting about?"

Me: "He keeps ignoring me."

Friend: "Well, he's pretty cold by nature."

Me: "Yeah, the guy's literally made of ice."
en.editorNote: Tell this during a cold snap — the colder it is outside, the funnier it lands
variations: Alt: Replace the snowman with a fridge — "He won't even open the door for me."

items.sourceNote: 受『冷vs熱誰比較快』經典dad joke啟發，改寫為台式情境劇版

zh-tw.content:
弟弟問我：「冷的跟熱的誰跑得比較快？」

我說：「熱的。」

弟弟：「為什麼？」

我：「因為冷的會被人『抓』到，熱的只會讓人『燙』到放手。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在家裡跟小朋友互動，講完記得自己先笑出聲
variations: 也可以反問：『那為什麼夏天比較難抓？因為太熱手了。』

en.content:
My little brother asked: "Which is faster, hot or cold?"

Me: "Hot."

Brother: "Why?"

Me: "Because you can catch a cold, but nobody ever catches a hot."
en.editorNote: Classic groaner for kids — laugh at your own joke first to set the mood
variations: Bonus: "And summer? Even harder to catch — it slips right through your fingers."

items.sourceNote: 受『月球餐廳沒氣氛』經典dad joke啟發，改寫為台式情境對話版

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我：「聽說月球上開了一家新餐廳？」

我說：「對啊，食物超好吃。」

朋友：「那為什麼沒人去？」

我：「因為完全沒氣氛——連空氣都沒有。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在約會挑餐廳的時候打岔，配合一臉認真表情更冷
variations: 延伸版：『而且服務生都飄來飄去的，連小費都收不到。』

en.content:
My friend asked: "Did you hear they opened a restaurant on the moon?"

Me: "Yeah, the food's amazing."

Friend: "So why is nobody going?"

Me: "No atmosphere — literally."
en.editorNote: Drop this when picking a date-night restaurant; deadpan delivery is essential
variations: Follow-up: "The waiters keep floating off before you can tip them."

items.sourceNote: 受台式『木瓜怕怕』papaya諧音梗啟發，改寫為水果店對話版

zh-tw.content:
我去水果攤問老闆：「哪種水果最膽小？」

老闆說：「我哪知道。」

我說：「木瓜啊，整天『怕怕』（papaya）。」

老闆：「那哪種最勇敢？」

我：「榴槤，全身都是刺，誰敢碰它。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在傳統市場跟長輩攀談，越冷反而越有人情味
variations: 也可以接：『那最會數學的呢？西瓜，因為它有西瓜子（種子=種類）。』

en.content:
I asked the fruit stand owner: "What fruit is the biggest scaredy-cat?"

Owner: "No idea."

Me: "A chicken-pea. It's always running."

Owner: "And the bravest?"

Me: "A durian. Covered in spikes — nobody dares touch it."
en.editorNote: Use this with kids at the grocery store; the worse it lands, the better
variations: Try: "What fruit is best at math? A pi-neapple."

items.sourceNote: 受『手錶皮帶=浪費時間』經典pun啟發，改寫為情侶送禮情境版

zh-tw.content:
女友生日，我送她一條皮帶，上面鑲了十二支手錶。

她問：「這是什麼？」

我說：「這叫『腰』費時間（waist of time），戴上去你就懂了。」

她：「我懂了——我跟你交往真的是浪費時間。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 千萬不要真的這樣送禮，當笑話講就好
variations: 也可以改成送朋友：『送你這個提醒你別再遲到了。』

en.content:
For my girlfriend's birthday, I gave her a belt with twelve watches stitched onto it.

She asked: "What is this?"

Me: "It's a waist of time. You'll get it eventually."

Girlfriend: "I already get it — dating you is a waste of time."
en.editorNote: Tell this one, don't actually do it; the punchline carries the gift
variations: Friend version: "It's so you stop being late."

items.sourceNote: 受『腳踏車太累倒下=two tired』經典pun啟發，改寫為加班族情境版

zh-tw.content:
下班時看到我的腳踏車倒在地上。

同事問：「你的車怎麼倒了？」

我說：「因為它太累了。」

同事：「車怎麼會累？」

我：「它有『兩個輪胎』（two tired），加班一整天當然撐不住。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在加班到崩潰的時候自嘲一下，越累越好笑
variations: 延伸版：『我比它更慘，我有兩個老闆（too boss-ed）。』

en.content:
After work I found my bike on the ground.

Coworker: "Why did your bike fall over?"

Me: "It was tired."

Coworker: "How does a bike get tired?"

Me: "It's two-tired. Long day at the office."
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating overtime humor; the more exhausted you sound, the better
variations: Follow-up: "I've got it worse — I'm two-bossed."

items.sourceNote: 受『火山我lava你』經典dad pun啟發，改寫為地理課對話版

zh-tw.content:
地理課上，老師問：「火山跟火山之間會說什麼？」

全班沉默。

老師：「我『熔』化你了（I lava you）。」

後排同學：「老師，那地震跟地震呢？」

老師：「他們不講話，他們直接『搖』感情。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合學生時代的破冰，老師講比學生講更冷
variations: 也可以改成颱風版：『颱風跟颱風會說什麼？我為你旋轉。』

en.content:
In geography class, the teacher asked: "What does one volcano say to another?"

The class went silent.

Teacher: "I lava you."

Student in the back: "And what do two earthquakes say?"

Teacher: "Nothing — they just shake on it."
en.editorNote: Peak classroom cringe; teachers telling this lands worse than students, which is the point
variations: Typhoon version: "What does one typhoon say to another? I'm spinning for you."

items.sourceNote: 受『甲殼/假咳』螃蟹諧音梗啟發，重寫為辦公室同事吐槽版

zh-tw.content:
同事一直咳，我關心地問：「你是不是感冒了？」

他搖頭：「沒有，我只是甲殼類動物。」

我：「你是螃蟹？」

他：「不然你以為我為什麼一直『假咳』？我在演給老闆看，這樣才能早點下班。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在週五下午跟同事互相取暖時講
variations: 也可以改成『我不是請假，我是請『蝦』』

en.content:
My coworker kept coughing. I asked: "You catching a cold?"

He shook his head: "Nope. I'm just a crustacean."

Me: "You're a crab?"

Him: "Why else would I be shellfish enough to fake-cough until the boss sends me home early?"
en.editorNote: Friday-afternoon office solidarity humor
variations: Swap in lobster: "I'm not sick, I just have a shellfish reason to leave."

items.sourceNote: 受『concentrate 柳橙汁』經典dad pun啟發，改寫為早餐桌親子對話

zh-tw.content:
早餐時，爸爸盯著柳橙汁的紙盒看了五分鐘。

媽媽：「你在看什麼？」

爸爸：「上面叫我『專心』（concentrate），我不敢動。」

小明：「爸，那旁邊那罐牛奶寫『全脂』，你要不要去健身房？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合假日早餐桌全家發呆時開講
variations: 也可以改成低脂版：『低脂在叫我減肥，我先吃完這片培根再說。』

en.content:
At breakfast, Dad stared at the orange juice carton for five minutes.

Mom: "What are you looking at?"

Dad: "It said 'concentrate.' I didn't want to break its focus."

Kid: "Dad, the milk says 'whole.' Should you go to the gym to feel complete?"
en.editorNote: Lazy weekend breakfast banter
variations: Low-fat version: "The label says low-fat. It's judging me. I'll finish this bacon first."

items.sourceNote: 受『窗戶開著電腦感冒』科技pun啟發，改寫為IT部門對話

zh-tw.content:
工程師打給IT：「我電腦一直在打噴嚏。」

IT：「電腦不會打噴嚏。」

工程師：「那為什麼我的Windows一直在彈出？」

IT沉默三秒：「先生，請您把電源拔掉重開，順便也把您拔掉重開。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在公司被IT電的時候自嘲，技術人聽得懂的冷
variations: 也可以改成『我的滑鼠在咳嗽，因為它有Bug（蟲）』

en.content:
Engineer calls IT: "My computer keeps sneezing."

IT: "Computers don't sneeze."

Engineer: "Then why do my Windows keep popping up?"

IT, after three seconds of silence: "Sir, please unplug the machine and reboot. Then please unplug yourself and reboot too."
en.editorNote: Self-roast humor for anyone who has annoyed their IT team
variations: Swap in: "My mouse keeps coughing — it must have a bug."

items.sourceNote: 受『榴槤流連忘返』水果諧音梗啟發，重寫為水果攤老闆對話版

zh-tw.content:
我去水果攤問老闆：「哪種水果最容易迷路？」

老闆：「榴槤。」

我：「為什麼？」

老闆：「因為流連忘返。」

我：「那哪種最會打招呼？」

老闆：「柿子，柿在哈囉。」

我：「那哪種水果最孝順？」

老闆：「你別問了，再問我要關門了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在水果攤、菜市場現場跟老闆互動時用
variations: 結尾可以加一句：『那最後一個答案是橘子，因為他很『局氣』。』

en.content:
I asked the fruit stand owner: "Which fruit gets lost most easily?"

Owner: "Durian."

Me: "Why?"

Owner: "Because it always lingers and never finds its way back."

Me: "Okay, which fruit is the friendliest?"

Owner: "Persimmon. Always saying 'simmon hi.'"

Me: "And which fruit is the most loyal son?"

Owner: "Ask one more and I'm closing the shop."
en.editorNote: Great for chatting up a fruit vendor in real life
variations: Add a closer: "The loyal one is the tangerine — he's always tangy with respect."

items.sourceNote: 受『星巴克=星八公克』經典諧音梗啟發，改寫為咖啡廳店員對話版

zh-tw.content:
我去咖啡廳跟店員說：「我要一杯月亮的重量。」

店員愣住：「蛤？」

我：「就八公克啊，星巴克。」

店員深呼吸：「先生，這裡是路易莎。」

我：「那給我一杯比較便宜的星星好了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合排隊買咖啡時跟朋友說，店員聽到會直接出戲
variations: 也可以改成：『我要一杯太陽的重量。』『多少？』『熱的，因為是熱情。』

en.content:
I walked into a coffee shop and told the barista: "I'd like one cup of the moon's weight."

Barista: "Excuse me?"

Me: "Eight grams. Like Starbucks. Star-eight-grams."

Barista, taking a deep breath: "Sir, this is a Dunkin'."

Me: "Then give me one cheap star instead."
en.editorNote: Good for whispering to a friend while waiting in line; the barista's face is half the joke
variations: Try the sun version: "I want one cup of the sun's weight." "How much?" "Hot — because it's full of warmth."

items.sourceNote: 受『anti-gravity book impossible to put down』經典dad pun啟發，改寫為書店場景版

zh-tw.content:
小華在書店翻了一本書翻了三小時，店員忍不住問：「同學，這本你看完要買嗎？」

小華：「不是我不想放下，是這本叫《反地心引力入門》。」

店員：「所以呢？」

小華：「物理上放不下啊。」

店員：「那旁邊那本《如何離開書店》要不要也參考一下？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在書店、圖書館跟朋友耍冷，也可以改成跟同學的下課對話
variations: 也可以改成《量子力學入門》：『因為我同時放下又沒放下。』

en.content:
Kevin had been flipping through the same book for three hours. The shop assistant finally asked: "Are you actually buying that one?"

Kevin: "It's not that I don't want to put it down. It's called 'Intro to Anti-Gravity.'"

Assistant: "And?"

Kevin: "Physically impossible."

Assistant: "Maybe try the one next to it: 'How to Leave the Bookstore.'"
en.editorNote: Bookstore-friendly cringe; works equally well in a school library
variations: Quantum version: 'Intro to Quantum Mechanics — I both put it down and didn't.'

items.sourceNote: 受『chicken coop two doors / sedan』雙關啟發，改寫為農場觀光導覽版

zh-tw.content:
我去農場觀光，導覽叔叔指著雞舍說：「你們看，這雞舍只有兩個門。」

小朋友舉手：「為什麼？」

叔叔：「因為如果有四個門，那就是『轎雞』了。」

小朋友：「那五個門呢？」

叔叔：「五門就是休旅雞，後面還能塞行李。」

旁邊的媽媽小聲對爸爸說：「我們下次改去看海好了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合家庭出遊時當車上閒聊，小朋友聽不懂、大人會嘆氣
variations: 也可以加一句：『那六個門呢？六門是貨雞，要載飼料的。』

en.content:
On a farm tour, the guide pointed at the chicken coop: "Notice it only has two doors."

A kid raised a hand: "Why?"

Guide: "Because if it had four, it'd be a sedan."

Kid: "What about five doors?"

Guide: "Then it's a hatchback — extra room for the eggs."

The mom whispered to the dad: "Next weekend, the beach. Just the beach."
en.editorNote: Family-road-trip energy; kids look confused, parents sigh
variations: Add: "Six doors? That's a delivery van — for the feed."

items.sourceNote: 受『secrets in cornfield, too many ears』經典pun啟發，改寫為情侶在玉米田散步版

zh-tw.content:
女友帶我去玉米田散步，浪漫地說：「我跟你講一個秘密喔。」

我：「不行，這裡不能講。」

女友：「為什麼？」

我：「你看四周，玉米都長『耳朵』了，被聽到怎麼辦？」

女友：「……我突然覺得單身好像也不錯。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合約會時講，講完做好被白眼的心理準備
variations: 也可以改成『不要在洋蔥旁邊講分手，會有人哭得比你更慘』

en.content:
My girlfriend took me for a walk through a cornfield and whispered: "Let me tell you a secret."

Me: "We can't talk about it here."

Her: "Why not?"

Me: "Look around. The corn has ears. Someone might overhear."

Her: "...You know, being single is starting to sound peaceful."
en.editorNote: Date-night material; brace yourself for an eye roll
variations: Onion version: "Don't break up next to onions — someone will cry harder than you."

items.sourceNote: 受『I told my Wi-Fi a joke, it didn't connect』科技梗啟發，改寫為咖啡廳遠端工作對話版

zh-tw.content:
我在咖啡廳遠端工作，對著筆電說了一個笑話。

隔壁桌：「你在跟誰講話？」

我：「我跟Wi-Fi講笑話。」

隔壁桌：「結果呢？」

我：「它沒連上。」

隔壁桌：「那你重開機看看。」

我：「我也想重開機，但人生不支援。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在咖啡廳工作崩潰、想休息卻不能休息時自嘲
variations: 也可以改成跟藍牙講：『我跟我的藍牙牙講祕密，結果它配對到隔壁桌。』

en.content:
I was working remotely at a cafe and told my laptop a joke.

Guy next to me: "Who are you talking to?"

Me: "I told my Wi-Fi a joke."

Him: "And?"

Me: "It didn't connect."

Him: "Try rebooting."

Me: "I'd reboot myself, but life doesn't support that."
en.editorNote: Self-deprecating cafe-worker humor for when you've stared at your screen too long
variations: Bluetooth version: "I told my Bluetooth a secret. It paired with the guy next to me."

items.sourceNote: 受『4×4=石榴』台式數字諧音梗啟發，改寫為國中數學課情境版

zh-tw.content:
數學課老師問：「四乘四等於多少？」

小明舉手：「石榴。」

老師：「答錯，是十六。」

小明：「老師，十六台語就是石榴。」

老師深呼吸：「那五乘五呢？」

小明：「二十五，台語叫『二五仔』，所以這題不能講，會被學長盯上。」

老師：「下課你留下來，我們來做更多『心算』練習。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在班上跟同學亂答數學題的時候講，越正經的場合越好笑
variations: 也可以接：『那七乘七呢？四十九，意思是『死就死』，這題我放棄。』

en.content:
Math teacher: "What's four times four?"

Kid raises hand: "A pomegranate."

Teacher: "Wrong. It's sixteen."

Kid: "In Taiwanese, sixteen sounds exactly like pomegranate."

Teacher, taking a long breath: "Fine. Five times five?"

Kid: "Twenty-five, but I can't say it out loud — it sounds like 'snitch' in Taiwanese."

Teacher: "Stay after class. We're doing more 'mental' arithmetic."
en.editorNote: Classroom cringe — works best when delivered with a totally straight face
variations: Add: "Seven times seven? Forty-nine, which sounds like 'whatever, I give up' in Taiwanese."

items.sourceNote: 受『slow tomato — ketchup』經典dad pun啟發，改寫為家庭夜市買鹽酥雞情境版

zh-tw.content:
全家去夜市買鹽酥雞，弟弟走得超慢。

爸爸催：「你快一點啦。」

弟弟：「我是番茄。」

爸爸：「番茄怎樣？」

弟弟：「番茄走很慢，但是會『跟上』（catch up = ketchup）。」

爸爸：「那我們是九層塔，先走了。」

弟弟：「為什麼？」

爸爸：「因為我們不等你，配什麼都香。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合全家逛夜市的時候講，把不耐煩變成笑話
variations: 也可以改成兄弟賽跑版：『我是番茄，你是美乃滋，我們在沙拉裡見。』

en.content:
The family was walking to the night market and my little brother kept lagging.

Dad: "Hurry up."

Bro: "I'm a tomato."

Dad: "And?"

Bro: "Tomatoes are slow, but I'll ketchup."

Dad: "Then we're basil. Going ahead."

Bro: "Why basil?"

Dad: "Because we don't wait for you, and we still pair with anything."
en.editorNote: Use this on a family stroll — turns impatience into a punchline
variations: Race version: "I'm a tomato, you're mayo. See you in the salad."

items.sourceNote: 受『cloud storage = sky-high prices』雲端梗啟發，改寫為新創公司會議室情境版

zh-tw.content:
新創公司開會，財務說：「我們的雲端儲存費用，又漲了。」

老闆：「為什麼？」

財務：「因為它叫『雲端』，價格當然飛天。」

工程師舉手：「那我們改用『地端』好了，至少摔下來不會痛。」

老闆：「那行銷部門呢？」

行銷：「我們在『雲深不知處』。」

老闆：「所以你們的進度也找不到？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在新創或軟體公司同事間互相吐槽，越貼近現實越冷
variations: 也可以接：『那資料庫呢？』『資料庫在地下室，因為它已經跌停了。』

en.content:
Startup meeting. Finance: "Our cloud storage bill went up again."

CEO: "Why?"

Finance: "It's the cloud — the prices are sky-high by definition."

Engineer: "Let's switch to ground storage. At least when it crashes, it doesn't hurt as much."

CEO: "And marketing?"

Marketing: "We're somewhere in the clouds, hard to locate."

CEO: "So your progress is also missing?"
en.editorNote: Startup meeting cringe; the closer to your real Monday, the colder it lands
variations: Add: "What about the database?" "In the basement — it already hit rock bottom."

items.sourceNote: 受『AI finishing my sentences — algo-rhythm』AI梗啟發，改寫為情侶吵架AI幫腔版

zh-tw.content:
女友氣噗噗：「你都不懂我！」

我看了一眼ChatGPT，它已經把我下一句打好了。

我念出來：「我懂，妳只是需要被理解。」

女友愣住：「你什麼時候變這麼會講話？」

我：「我跟AI找到我們的『演算節奏』了。」

女友：「那你跟它過好了。」

我：「等等，它建議我現在道歉。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在朋友面前吐槽現代戀愛的狀況，越平靜的語氣越冷
variations: 也可以接：『AI說我應該買花，我說沒預算，AI回我：那就買感情股。』

en.content:
Girlfriend, fuming: "You never understand me!"

I glanced at ChatGPT — it had already drafted my next line.

I read it aloud: "I do. You just need to feel heard."

Her, stunned: "Since when do you talk like that?"

Me: "My AI and I finally found our algo-rhythm."

Her: "Then go date it."

Me: "Hold on — it's telling me to apologize right now."
en.editorNote: Modern-relationship cringe; deliver in a flat, deadpan tone
variations: Add: "AI told me to buy flowers. I said I'm broke. It said: invest in emotional stock."

items.sourceNote: 受『一顆星星八克／星巴克』台式諧音梗啟發，改寫為早餐店點餐情境版

zh-tw.content:
我去早餐店點餐，老闆娘問：「要喝什麼？」

我：「一顆星星多重？」

老闆娘：「八克。」

我：「所以一杯星巴克。」

老闆娘：「我們這沒賣星巴克。」

我：「那給我兩顆星星。」

老闆娘：「你給我滾去隔壁。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在連鎖早餐店亂講的時候用，老闆越無奈越好笑
variations: 也可以接：『那三顆星星呢？』『二十四克，所以是米其林。』

en.content:
At the breakfast spot, the owner asked: "What to drink?"

Me: "How much does a star weigh?"

Owner: "...About eight grams."

Me: "Then I'll have one Starbucks."

Owner: "We don't carry Starbucks."

Me: "Fine, give me two stars."

Owner: "Get out. Go next door."
en.editorNote: Order-counter chaos; the more deadpan you are, the colder it lands
variations: Add: "What about three stars?" "Twenty-four grams — that's a Michelin."

items.sourceNote: 受『new mouse — we just weren't clicking』IT梗啟發，改寫為公司分手談話情境版

zh-tw.content:
同事很沮喪：「我跟我女友分手了。」

我：「為什麼？」

同事：「我們之間就是『點不到』。」

我：「所以你買了新滑鼠？」

同事：「沒，我換了新女友。」

我：「那舊的呢？」

同事：「歸還IT部門了，他們說會重新安裝韌體。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合辦公室加班崩潰時跟工程師同事講，越冷越紓壓
variations: 也可以接：『那鍵盤呢？』『鍵盤還在，因為它從來不回我。』

en.content:
Coworker, glum: "I broke up with my girlfriend."

Me: "Why?"

Him: "We just weren't clicking."

Me: "So you got a new mouse?"

Him: "No, a new girlfriend."

Me: "What happened to the old one?"

Him: "Returned her to IT. They said they'd reinstall the firmware."
en.editorNote: Late-night office cringe; perfect for engineer coworkers
variations: Add: "And the keyboard?" "Still here. It never replies anyway."

items.sourceNote: 受『seagulls fly over sea / over bay = bagels』經典dad pun啟發，改寫為家庭海邊野餐情境版

zh-tw.content:
全家在海邊野餐，妹妹問爸爸：「為什麼海鷗只飛海上，不飛海灣？」

爸爸：「因為飛海灣就變成『貝果』了。」

媽媽默默把三明治收起來。

妹妹：「那為什麼牠們不飛沙灘？」

爸爸：「飛沙灘就變成『沙其瑪』。」

哥哥舉手：「那如果牠們飛回家呢？」

爸爸：「那就變成晚餐。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在野餐現場耍冷，爸爸越正經，全家越想假裝不認識他
variations: 也可以改成『飛山上就變成滷肉飯』『山的諧音是攤，攤就是賣的』

en.content:
Family beach picnic. Little sister asks Dad: "Why do seagulls fly over the sea, not the bay?"

Dad: "Because if they flew over the bay, they'd be bagels."

Mom quietly puts the sandwiches away.

Sister: "What if they fly over the sand?"

Dad: "Sand-wiches."

Big brother raises a hand: "And if they fly home?"

Dad: "Then they're dinner."
en.editorNote: Beach-picnic dad energy; the straighter Dad's face, the more the family pretends not to know him
variations: Add: "Over the rocks? They'd be 'rock-and-roll' chicken."

items.sourceNote: 受咖啡梗『daily grind / espresso yourself』啟發，改寫為早晨辦公室同事尬聊版

zh-tw.content:
星期一早上，同事頂著黑眼圈說：「我覺得我撐不到中午。」

我：「你需要『拿鐵』勇氣。」

同事：「不要冷笑話。」

我：「那來杯『美式』，很直接。」

同事翻白眼：「拜託閉嘴。」

我：「那加一份濃縮，幫你『精神焠鍊』。」

同事：「我辭職。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合星期一早上在茶水間講，越早講同事越想請假
variations: 可以再接：『不然你來杯摩卡？因為跟你聊天讓我『莫卡』奈何。』
改成主管版：『你需要的不是咖啡，是離職單。』

en.content:
Monday morning. Coworker, with raccoon eyes: "I'm not gonna survive till noon."

Me: "You need a latte courage."

Him: "Please don't."

Me: "How about an Americano? Very straightforward."

Him, eye-rolling: "Stop talking."

Me: "Add a shot of espresso. Really espresso yourself."

Him: "I quit."
en.editorNote: Best deployed in the office break room before 9am; the earlier, the more painful
variations: Add: "Or a mocha? Because mocha-ing fun of you is my hobby."
Boss version: "You don't need coffee. You need a resignation letter."

items.sourceNote: 受台灣諧音冷笑話『動物為什麼...』題型啟發，改寫為動物園親子情境版

zh-tw.content:
兒子在動物園問：「爸爸，為什麼長頸鹿都不講話？」

爸爸：「因為牠們『脖子長』，話到嘴邊都涼了。」

兒子：「那為什麼河馬都張嘴？」

爸爸：「因為牠在『河』你哈囉。」

兒子：「那為什麼大象用鼻子吸水？」

爸爸：「因為牠覺得喝水太『象』話。」

兒子：「我想去找媽媽。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合假日親子遊動物園，孩子越想逃跑爸爸越開心
variations: 可以接：『那企鵝為什麼穿西裝？』『因為牠要去開會，會議叫做極地高峰會。』
改成爺爺版：每題後面都加一句『你阿公以前都這樣講笑話』

en.content:
At the zoo, son asks: "Dad, why don't giraffes ever talk?"

Dad: "By the time the words climb up that neck, they're stale."

Son: "Why do hippos keep their mouths open?"

Dad: "They're hippo-thesizing."

Son: "Why do elephants suck up water with their trunks?"

Dad: "Because drinking it normal-style would be ir-elephant."

Son: "I want Mom."
en.editorNote: Weekend zoo trip energy; the more your kid tries to walk away, the better it's working
variations: Add: "Why do penguins wear tuxedos?" "They're heading to a formal ice-cebreaker."
Grandpa edition: end each line with "your grandpa used to tell these too."

items.sourceNote: 受『someone glued my deck of cards / can't deal』經典one-liner啟發，改寫為室友抱怨情境版

zh-tw.content:
室友哀號：「有人把我整副撲克牌黏在一起了。」

我：「誰幹的？」

室友：「不知道，但我現在『無牌可打』。」

我：「那你不就『黑白來』？」

室友：「我是說真的，這是要我怎麼面對？」

我：「面對牌背啊。」

室友把抱枕丟過來：「滾出我房間。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合跟室友半夜耍冷，越冷越助眠
variations: 可以再接：『沒關係，你還有一手好牌——叫做手氣。』
改成桌遊社版：『所以下週社團活動，你是不是要請大家吃飯？』

en.content:
Roommate, wailing: "Someone glued my whole deck of cards together."

Me: "Who did it?"

Him: "No idea, but now I literally can't deal."

Me: "Sounds like the situation is stuck."

Him: "I'm serious. How am I supposed to face this?"

Me: "Face down. Like the cards."

Roommate throws a pillow: "Get out."
en.editorNote: Late-night roommate cringe; the colder it is, the faster they fall asleep
variations: Add: "Don't worry, you still have a hand to play — your sense of humor."
Board-game-club version: "So next meeting, you're buying snacks, right?"

items.sourceNote: 受『two-tired bicycle / hospital stable horses』類型dad joke啟發，改寫為健身房教練尬聊情境版

zh-tw.content:
在健身房，教練問我：「今天要練什麼？」

我：「練習放棄。」

教練：「不行，你要『重訓』新做人。」

我：「我已經『重』夠了。」

教練：「那來深蹲。」

我：「我『蹲』在家就好。」

教練：「滾去跑步機，跑到你不想講笑話為止。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在健身房偷懶時用來氣教練，越冷越能拖延訓練
variations: 可以再接：『那做仰臥起坐？』『我『仰』天長嘆就好。』
改成瑜伽版：『下犬式我會，因為我已經是條狗了。』

en.content:
At the gym, trainer asks: "What are we training today?"

Me: "Training to give up."

Trainer: "No. You need to lift yourself together."

Me: "I've already lifted enough — like, my expectations."

Trainer: "Squats. Now."

Me: "I'd rather squat at home. Same form, more snacks."

Trainer: "Treadmill. Run until you stop telling jokes."
en.editorNote: Perfect for stalling during a personal training session; the colder, the longer the rest
variations: Add: "Sit-ups?" "I prefer sit-downs."
Yoga version: "I'm great at downward dog — I've been a dog all morning."

items.sourceNote: 受『computer cold / Windows open』經典tech pun啟發，改寫為MIS同事互嗆情境版

zh-tw.content:
MIS同事衝進辦公室抖個不停：「我電腦怎麼這麼冷？」

我抬頭看他：「你『視窗』沒關吧？」

他：「⋯⋯你是說 Windows ？」

我：「對，全開的那種。」

他：「我那是分頁，不是視窗。」

我：「那你就是『分頁失溫』。」

他默默走回工位拔網路線。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在開放式辦公室冷氣太強時，跟工程師同事尬聊
variations: 可以再接：『要不要我幫你重灌？人生那種。』
改成主管版：『難怪你 KPI 一直『當機』。』

en.content:
